amps.name: 5F1 TWEED CHAMPLIFIER
body: MODEL: Narrow-panel Fender Tweed Champ, 5F1
Orig. Cab: 1x6”or 8” Alnico (Jensen P8T/Oxford 8EV)
DynaCab: 1x8 5F1 Tweed
Controls: Volume (Drive)
Preamp tubes: 12AX7A Syl
Power tubes: 6V6
Tonestack location: PRE
Notes: Tiny speaker attributes to the boxy sound
Low output & speaker size make amp break up early
Crank volume & you’ll understand why expression “farty bass” exists. Cut switch helps tame low end
Popular recording amp allegedly used on Clapton’s “Layla” & Joe Walsh’s “Rocky Mountain Way”
Some of these old amps, like 5F1, produces a sub- octave & why some value it. Makes sound full/warm
This amp exhibits a unique breakup characteristic due to single-end design & simple circuit
Tips: “Stick to single coils, humbucker output signal is too strong for the Champ to handle” - yek
Amp only has Volume control. For authenticity, only adjust Input Drive in Amp block
Amp has 2 inputs. Fractal’s Fender amp models are always based on the input with highest level. To get equivalent of lowest input, set Input Trim in to 0.500

amps.name: 5F1 TWEED EC CHAMPLIFIER
body: Model: 2011 Fender EC Vibro-Champ
Orig. Cab: Fender 1x8
DynaCab: 1x8 5F1 Tweed
Controls: Volume, Tremolo effect
Power tubes: 6V6
Tonestack location: PRE
Notes: Circuit’s slightly different than Orig. 5F1, has cathode bypass caps, giving it more gain

Single-ended power amp with negative feedback/ bias tremolo, so subtle is only possibility. Bias tremolo works by modulating quiescent operating point of power tubes. With single-ended power amp & negative fdbk, gain doesn’t change much changing operating point, tremolo is subtle. Bias tremolo works best on amps with little to no negative fdbk

amps.name: 5F8 TWEED BRIGHT
body: Model: Keith Urban’s ‘59 narrow-panel, high-power Fender Tweed Twin, 5F8, Bright channel
Orig. Cab: Fender 2x12 P12N, Urban’s cab: Two-Rocks
DynaCab: 2x12 Double Verb
Controls: Presence, Middle, Bass, Treble, Bright/Normal Inputs Volume
Preamp tubes: 12AX7A Syl
Power tubes: 6L6/5881
Tonestack location: POST
Notes: All Fender models, except ‘59 Bassman & 5F8 Tweed don’t have cathode followers
Tweed amps typically break up earlier than later models & known for warm sounding distortion
Narrow panel refers to panels around speaker grill, narrower vs earlier “wide panel” models
Marked start of period when Fender amps were loud enough to fill large rooms themselves, not needing sound system, enough headroom to stay clean if loud
Said to sound similar to Bassman, but warmer & larger
Early non-reverb model (different from later models with reverb, like Twin Reverb)
Famous Twin-Amp players: Keith Richards, Clapton, late Rory Gallagher, Bonamassa, Steven Seagall & David Gilmour

Tips: “amp doesn’t stay as pristine clean at higher volume like Twin Reverb, especially with humbuckers. Still has lot of clean headroom. With single coils, crank Input Drive in Block to overdrive it. Even then, it doesn’t distort a ton, compression increases & tone is fatter, in a nice way” - yek
“Amp not as bright vs later Twin Reverb. In fact, when combined with its stock cab, it’s dark sounding. Be like Keith Urban & turn up Treble & Presence & turn down Bass” - yek
No Master Vol, keep Master control cranked for authenticity
See below for Keith Urban & Joe Bonamassa’s amp settings

Amp originally had 2 10” speakers. Prefer 10” speakers? Try stock cab 2x10 Super Tweed for different tone

Brown Fenders cleaner vs Tweeds, warmer/dirtier vs blackfaces
Bassman was designed for bass players, but adopted by guitar players, Concert was a guitar amp, was used by living legend Carol Kaye for bass. Like Super, a lot love amp’s vibrato effect
Tips: Amp has lot of clean headroom & bass. Reduce bass & crank Input Drive to get a mild “brown” overdrive
“Lotta clean headroom & bass. Have to really crank it with single coils to get to mild overdrive territory.” – yek

amps.name: ‘59 BASSGUY BRIGHT

Preamp tubes: 12AX7A (Syl)
Power tubes: 6L6/5881
Tonestack location: POST
Notes: Known for warm distortion
Tweeds usually break up earlier
Bassman was originally a bass amp, but was adopted as guitar amp. Simple, pure, raw tone, “Grandfather of rock n roll” amp
Famous Bassman players: Clapton, Hendrix, Brian Setzer, & more
Marshall copied Design for JTM-45
Bassman reworked using OG 59 Bassman 5F6-A, 12AY7 input buffer, less gain, uses 12AX7A (7025 actually, same thing)
Bassman & Deluxe Tweed models both modeled with 12AY7 input buffer. Preamp Tube Type control only affects tubes after input buffer
Input to 2nd 12AX7 is summing node, both Volumes affect signal,  Bright input, Normal controls volume some & changes freq response
Tips: Turn down Bass & turn up mids
Or try Bass/Treble at 0 & Mid/Presence at 10
Lot of bass & starts distorting with volume increased (Input Drive)
Can do it all: clean, funk, soul, blues & rock’n’roll
Authenticity: keep MV control cranked
Try cranked mids & Bass to 0
Or Bass/Treble at 0 & crank Presence/Mid
“For some vintage fun, put Face Fuzz before amp, or control low end with a Treble Booster” -yek
“4x10 Bassguy good cab for Fender models with 10” speakers.” - yek

amps.name: ‘59 BASSGUY JUMPED

Notes: See 008 ‘59 BASSGUY for more info on this amp

See 008 ‘59 BASSGUY for more info

amps.name: ‘65 BASSGUY BASS
body: Model: Fender Bassman ‘65 blackface, AB165
Orig. Cab: 2x12” Jensens
DynaCab: 2x12 65 Bassguy
Controls: Volume, Treble, Bass
Preamp tubes: 7025 (12AX7A Syl)
Power tubes: 6L6/5881
Tonestack location: PRE

Notes: “What does blackface Bassman sound like? Describing tone’s best done with common reference point, ie blackface AB763 amps, like Super/Twin Reverb. Bassman known for simple, pure & raw tone. 50w loud with power supply & output transformers approx size of Super Reverb & Bandmaster. Bass channel of AB165/AA864 is much mellow/deep vs normal channel of any AB763 amps, since it’s voiced for bass guitars. Bassman bass channel features deep switch, can remove lowest freqs, practical when playing loud & using different speaker cabs & need to adjust lower bass. Bassman sounds “browner” vs AB763-amps, specifically AB165 & more preamp distortion/ mids when Volume is turned up. AB763 amps have less preamp gain due to use of only 1 half/triode (V4 12ax7) in 2nd gain stage vs both halfs/triodes (V2 12ax7) in Bassman AB165. Bassman amps have more mids vs AB763

amps.name: ‘65 BASSGUY NORMAL
body: Notes: See 012 ‘65 Bassguy for more info
“Dweezil’s Bassman has 2 channels too. Normal channel is “normal” & corresponds to 65 Bassguy Normal model”
“Normal channel is more relevant for guitar players. In AA864 Bassman, it’s cleaner vs AB165, as it has no additional gain stage”

amps.name: 1959SLP JUMPED
body: Model: 100W Marshall Super Lead Plexi 1959 RI, jumpered chnls
Orig. Cab: Marshall 4x12 (G12M, G12H)
DynaCab: 4x12 1960TV
Controls: Vol Normal/Treble, Bass, Mid, Treble, Presence
Preamp tubes: ECC83
Power tubes: EL34
Tonestack location: POST
Notes: 1959SLP Plexi is the amp that defines classic rock tone g820ohm cathode resistor (1st stage), 0.68uF cap on 2nd triode
These amps are referred to as “Plexi” because of gold Plexiglass front panel, later replaced with gold aluminum

“One of 1st mods made to Plexi Marshalls is “clip bright cap”. Bright cap varies over years, rumor is it depended on what was lying around shop. Defaults to bright cap in circuit. Bright off effectively clips bright cap. Bright cap in Marshalls can be very bright/harsh. But, cranking Master, may find extra brightness helps compensate for power amp getting darker.”

Some players of real Plexi use patch cables to jumper inputs: 2nd input of channel 1 goes to 1st input of channel 2, enables having benefits of both channels at once. This particular amp model has 2 Drive controls, set at same position, or keep Normal Drive lower than Treble Drive

Numerous guitar heroes played some kind of Plexi: Pete Townsend, Jimi Hendrix, Jimmy Page, Angus Young, Yngwie Malmsteen, Jeff Beck & of course Eddie Van Halen g[Response to complaints about “fizzy” Plexis] “What you’re hearing is output transformer high freq resonance. Old Plexis didn’t do this, as they had good transformers. New transformers are crappy & resonate at cutoff freq, as they’re underdamped, causing raspy, fizzy texture to distortion. Transformer resonance is adjustable, but parameter isn’t exposed to user. When you turn it up, you get that same squishy rasp that you’re hearing. For Plexis, I have damping set to flat, with no resonance, since that’s how our vintage Plexis measure. 50W is little underdamped, but people complain about raspy high freqs so I erred on safe side.”
“Way Plexi is supposed to sound, due to cathode follower. Raspiness helps cut through mix. I own 3, they’re fizzy.”
“Don’t be afraid to turn bass 100% down or treble 100% up, just like actual amp. Ex: on Normal channel of Plexi, most people turn bass way down, otherwise it’s too flubby.”
Plexi is raw, unrefined, honest & touch sensitive. Hit power chord & feel explosion of sound in face & body
Eddie Van Halen cranked all volume & tone controls on his Plexi & to emulate his Variac: set the parameter “AC Voltage (Variac)” at 75 or so in the amp model

“Modding Marshalls for higher gain, sometimes modder decrease input freq response to reduce noise/fizz. To replicate: lower input EQ’s high-cut freq, or use high-shelf filter (little more subtle). Another technique: put capacitor across 2nd-to- last triode stage (Triode 1 Plate Freq). Reduce to ~2kHz to start, typical of amps like SLO100, etc”
“Distortion from power amp, tone controls don’t do much”

Why’s Plexi become such a popular amp? “1st/most important perhaps is sense of dynamics & rich harmonics. No large- production amp created before/since early Marshall plexis have been able to capture feel of player through varying degrees of dynamics/color from (mostly) EL34-based tube circuits of these Marshalls. Call it a lucky accident with Marshall, but they created amps that really responded well to guitars played through them. Amps deliver wide palette of distortion color, rich, full, powerful & timeless in tone. Takes some time to really get used to playing basic amp, like a Marshall plexi. No multi channels, reverb or effects & no MV controls. Turn up amp, play hard & get tone fullness & smooth distortion. Back off playing, amp will respond/move to lighter shades of OD. Roll down guitar volume a touch & get warmed up clean sound. There’s nothing like ability to feel set of power & preamp tubes overdriving together musically.”–Legendary Tones

Like original Plexi, 1959SLP doesn’t have a Master, so keep Master control in amp model cranked. Without a MV control, Plexi amps rely on power amp distortion. To achieve a nice overdriven rock tone, you need to turn up a Plexi a lot. This makes it a very loud amp in real life g100 watt Plexi sounds more open, bigger & more “kerrang”
Cabs: ’69 4x12 Basketweave, with 2x 25w Greenbacks (mic’d) & 2x Weber Cerwin Vega copies (try OH Marshall 1966B), mic’d with Cascade Fat Head
Tips: Don’t hesitate to turn Bass all the way down, turn up Middle/Treble, or crank everything, like Eddie Van Halen
“My settings for typical Plexi tone: Bass 2, Mid 8, Treble 7.5, Presence to taste.”
Boston/Rockman sound: Plexi Jump, everything @ 7, Filter block in front, Peak type, Freq~800Hz, Q 0.707, Gain 12dB

If you like to experiment, turn up Supply Sag in the amp model for more compression. Note that high Sag settings may cause “ghosts notes”, similar to the real amp at high volume levels (watch the interview with J.D. Simo). If you don’t want ghosts notes, set Supply Type to DC
Another tweak: switch on Boost to slam amp model’s input stage, for more gain

Can’t discuss Marshall amps & leave out speakers, in particular: greenbacks. Early Marshalls were paired with 4x12” cabinets with Celestion G12Ms, have sweet midrange & good bass reproduction. Rear of speakers was green & nickname “greenbacks” was born. They’re the reason that 4x12 cabinets were designed: G12M was only 20 of 25w, need 4 to prevent blowing them up
Another popular speaker: G12 Heritage (G12H). 30w Celestion speaker that compresses less vs greenback & has flatter response (the Hendrix speaker)
JD Simo’s Settings: Bright Channel, Bass:0, Mid:10, Treble:10, Volume:10, Presence: to taste (~5-6)

amps.name: 1959SLP NORMAL
body: Model: 100W Marshall Super Lead Plexi 1959 RI, Normal chnl
Notes: See 014 1959SLP  for more info

amps.name: 1959SLP TREBLE
body: Model: 100W Marshall Super Lead Plexi ‘59 RI, Treble channel

Treble channel input, which can be painfully bright. Popular mod of a real Plexi is clipping bright cap. Amp block, it’s easy to do: turn off Bright, or adjust Bright Cap value (turning off Bright results in noticeable loss of amp gain)

amps.name: 1987X JUMPED
body: Model: 50W Marshall 1987X reissue, jumpered channels
Orig. Cab: Marshall 4x12 (G12M, G12H)
DynaCab: 4x12 1960TV
Controls: Volume Normal/Treble, Bass, Mid, Treble, Presence
Preamp tubes: ECC83
Power tubes: EL34
Tonestack location: POST
Notes: More aggressive vs ’60s Plexis, much more of ’72 period, heavy, sweet/aggressive tone
Doesn’t have 0.68uF cap on last triode (smoother distortion)

“Our reference amp has no bright cap. Looks like it was that way from factory & no signs it was removed. Location in circuit board is empty & solder joints don’t appear to have been disturbed.” g1987X model based on reissue 1987, doesn’t have bright cap. Use Plexi 50W model for vintage sound
Plexi is raw, unrefined, honest & touch sensitive. Hit a power chord through a Plexi & feel the explosion of sound in your face & body
Numerous guitar heroes played a Plexi: Pete Townsend, Jimi Hendrix, Jimmy Page, Angus Young, Yngwie Malmsteen, Jeff Beck & of course Eddie Van Halen
So in what aspect is a 50w Plexi different from 100w? Not a lot in volume, 50-watter is also very loud & not a lot in gain either. 50w Plexi is a little more aggressive & compressed

“While 50w 1987X head shares same Plexi front/rear panel as 1959SLP, has its own distinctive sonic personality. More aggressive vs ’60s Plexis, 1987X is much more of ’72 period, with heady, sweet/aggressive tone. These tonal characteristics are what define this much respected, all-valve head.” – Marshall g1987X doesn’t have a Master, keep MV control in amp model cranked. Without MV control, Plexi amps rely on power amp distortion. To achieve nice OD rock tone, need to turn it up a lot, makes it a very loud amp in real life

Can’t discuss Marshall amps & leave out speakers, in particular: greenbacks. Early Marshalls were paired with 4x12” cabinets with Celestion G12Ms, sweet midrange & good bass reproduction, reason 4x12 cab were designed: G12M was only 20 or 25w, need 4 to prevent blowing up
Another popular speaker: G12 Heritage (G12H). 30w Celestion speaker compresses less vs greenback & flatter response (Hendrix speaker)
“Sounds/feels juicy. Versatile amp, can handle anything from funk to hard rock”
“1 of my favorite Plexis, slightly darker, juicy tone”
Tips: Bass heavy, may needa turn it down
Has 2 Drive controls. Set the same or Normal Drive lower than Treble Drive
If you like to experiment, turn up Supply Sag in amp model for more compression. Note: high Sag settings may cause “ghosts notes”, similar to amp at high lvls
No ghosts notes: set Supply Type to DC
Another: switch Boost on to slam input stage (more gain)

The blue channel has much more gain

Has Master, so amp’s distortion created by preamp tubes, not power amp. Master Volume works in power amp section, still very important to tone/feel. Decide what setting works best, default is a good point to start

“IMO, very good amp. Build-quality is great, design’s very good. Similar to previous versions, but voiced bit different. Personally, I think all 5150’s have more gain stages than necessary, makes for unnecessary failure points but “OMG, this amp has so many toobz, it must sound awesome” marketing makes it understandable. Incredibly heavy.”

amps.name: AC-20 12AX7 BASS
body: Model: Morgan AC20 Deluxe, Bass model
Orig. Cab: Morgan 1x12 (G12 Alnico Blue)
DynaCab: 1x12 AC20
Controls: Volume, Cut, Sun/Moon switch (Bright), Brilliance switch (Bass Cut), EF86/12AX7 switch
Preamp tubes: 12AX7A (Syl)
Power tubes: EL84
Tonestack location: POST
Notes: 12AX7 is glassy & has sparkle
Based on amp with power scaling at max power, effectively disables it (& sounds best IMO)”
“I think AC-20 does Vox thing better than Vox”

“AC30’s have poorly designed cathode follower. Bias point’s wrong & crushes 1 signal side considerably. IMO either cath. resistor should be 100K or 200K plate resistor of previous stage. Clipping is extremely asymmetric, too much IMO. Little asymmetry is good/warms things up, too much has excessive even harmonics, makes things fuzzy/indistinct. Clipping’s so asymmetric, almost half-wave rectifier

Can tweak by adjusting Preamp Bias point &/or lower Cathode Follower Compress, or lower Harmonics value, reduces asymmetric distortion, but ODs phase inverter, causes blocking distortion via excess bias excursion

Also try increasing Grid Clipping, adds some headroom. Start via Bias point. AC30 are sensitive to tube type & part tolerances, tiny change make big difference. Default bias point is based on Mullard ECC83 tubes. I prefer AC- 20, it doesn’t have cathode follower & doesn’t suffer from these problems. Cathode follower in AC30 doesn’t do all that much. Normally, use cathode follower to pre-set a low-impedance source to tone stack, but in AC30 doesn’t have a great load anyways.”
“One reason I like AC-20 is it runs tubes hotter & exhibits less crossover distortion.”

“Sun/Moon=Bright Switch. Bass/Treble switch is different amp models. EF86 Bass model is Bass/Treble in Bass position, back switch in EF86 position & Bright switch on model is Sun/ Moon switch on amp.”
“Selected AC-20, heard lot of ghost notes, compared to old algo, figured it couldn’t be right. Hooked up an AC-20 & sure enough, ghost notes galore at same settings.”
“Delicate tone, intimate/small sound (good)”

“Normal channel on Vox is closest to Morgan. Brilliant is Top Boost, has tone stack & mid cut. Normal has no tone stack. Can’t compare Morgan AC20 to AC30 Brilliant & say 1 sounds better vs other. Totally different preamp/sound, AC20 is better amp/components/transformers”

“A deceptively simple amp, capable of early 60s British tones & more. EF86 tube help make range of tones from cleanest of EL84 chime to ferocious high gain growl. Power level control allows dialing in right volume, not sacrificing tone like some MV do. AC20 was created to solve modern guitar player amp issues, ability to stay true to vintage tonality, equally at home, in studio, or gigs. Seen on stages of Jeff Tweedy, Tim McGraw, The Band Perry & Toshi Yanagi (Jimmy Kimmel). Plug in & notice familiar chime/compression of Class A EL84 driven output. Amp’s extreme touch-sensitivity allow you to coax clean tones, even at highest of gain settings. Quoting Keith Armstrong (Chief Engineer at Mix LA) “It’s impossible to get a bad sound out of this amp!” AC20 Deluxe uses 3 separate switches to give all the original early 60’s EL84 tonalities, Normal, Brilliant & EF86. Handwired in USA.” -Morgan
“When I need VOXish sound, tend to choose AC-20. A gem in amp collection”
Tips: “Like real amp, I dial in bit of Hi-Cut”
For authenticity, keep tone controls default, except Hi-Cut. Try EF86 models with single- coils & 12AX7 with humbuckers
Amp models have Bright switch enabled. If too bright, turn it off or adjust Hi-Cut
Use very low Drive settings for clean tone
Chimey AC30 sound: EQ before/cut mids
Pairs well with G12H or Alnico Gold speakers

amps.name: ANGLE SEVERE 1
body: Model: Engl Savage 120 (Channel 2 Contour switch off, boosts lower midrange ~500Hz, for warm tone)
Orig. Cab: Engl 4x12 (V30, V60)
DynaCab: 4x12 Recto Straight
Controls: Gain (input sensitivity), Lead (distortion), Lead Boost switch, Contour switch, Bass, Mid, Treble, Master, Presence, Depth Boost switch
Preamp tubes: ECC83
Power tubes: 6550
Tonestack location: POST
Notes: Has loads of preamp gain g[Presence]50-100%=presence boost & 50-0%=cut
“Presence network reduces negative feedback as control is turned down causing volume increase”

“Depth control on amp is a switch. Set Depth to either 0 or 10 to duplicate behavior. Depth controlled by switch goes back to Peavey patent for pot to control depth. With pot for depth, had to pay royalties, so amp makers used switch to get around this. Believe patent’s expired, so newer amps often have knob. Model has added resistor & Presence control behave more predictably.”

“Input Drive rotated clockwise increases gain amount, tone shifts from treble/upper mid emphasis, for up front sparkling tone, to lower mid/ bass emphasis, for thick, meaty tone. Rotating knob clockwise, OD increases gain amount & no tonal balance change. Different Input Drive/OD combos has big effect on amp response & personality of instrument. Make any guitar sound mellow, fat, soulful or aggressive.” g2 gain controls: Gain & Lead (Input Drive & OD), 1st sets sensitivity & 2nd sets distortion
Models with separate Input Drive & OD controls always feel elastic & spongy, a real joy to play.
Ritchie Blackmore’s signature amp based on Savage
Famous Engl players: Steve Morse, Thin Lizzy, etc

High gain amp with Master, amp’s distortion created by preamp tubes, not in power amp. MV works in power amp section, important to tone & feel. Decide what settings works best, default is good start point

Tips: Engl uses Celestion V30/V60 speakers. Many V30s stock cabs. 4x12 German V30 is IR of Engl 4x12 PRO cab with V30s. Red Wirez stock cabs captured using neutral microphone, consider adding mic model in Cab block
For even more gain, engage Boost switch

amps.name: ANGLE SEVERE 2
body: Model: Engl Savage 120 (Chnl 2 Contour on, boost from 1200Hz & lower mid cut, for transparency)
Note: See 031  Angle Severe for more info

amps.name: ARCHEAN
body: Model: 100W PRS Archon (Lead chnl, bright off)
Orig. Cab: Celestion V-Type
DynaCab: 4x12 Friedman GB
Controls: MV, Bass, Mid, Treble, Bright, Vol, Depth, Presence
Preamp tubes: 12AX7
Power tubes: 6L6
Tonestack location: POST
Notes: “Crank MV on real amp. Preamp very dry, to get juices flowing, power amp needs to be working hard (also smooths out that funky midrange)”

“I think part of reason Archon needs cranked is due to power supply is too stiff. It’s got 6(!!!) big caps filtering B+, too much IMO, unless at arena volumes. If I made a mod, it’d be a switch to disconnect 4 caps, supply is more bouncy at moderate volumes, maybe a tube rectifier to add some more sponge.”
“Great sounding amp! Beefy cross between Marshall & Mesa.” - Tom Johnston, Doobie Bros.
“Not many amps have that warm punchy gain.” - Fredrik Akesson, Opeth

“Delivers crystal clear clean tone at unbelievable volume! On stage, I crank master & dial preamp for punchy sound that responds to notes with extreme clarity. Great for allowing pedals to color tone, while maintaining unparalleled richness & sparkle, without any breakup from amp” - Donna Grantis, Prince
“Real exciting amp. Whether needing nice bell like clean, rock crunch or super saturated metal tone, can do it all!” - Dustie Waring, Between the Buried & Me
“Delivers best clean & dirty channels I’ve ever heard in one amp. Just plug in & play.” - Emil Werstler

amps.name: ARCHEAN BRIGHT
body: Model: 100W PRS Archon (Lead Channel, bright on)
Note: See 033  Archean for more info

amps.name: ARCHEAN CLEAN
body: Model: 100W PRS Archon (Lead Channel, bright on)
Note: See 033  Archean for more info

amps.name: ATOMICA HIGH
body: Model: Cameron Atomica (high gain input w/ add’l gain stage)
Orig. Cab: cab with G12H speakers
DynaCab: 4x12 5153
Controls: Thump(Depth), Presence, Bass, Mid, Treble, MV, Gain(Drive), Edge(Bright), 3 Gain Voices
Preamp tubes: 12AX7A (JJ)
Power tubes: EL34
Tonestack location: POST
Notes: “Atomica can tolerate high MV, as it’s a Jose-style MV”, imparts very high source resistance to tone stack, causes much more insertion loss, therefore lower drive level into power amp”
“Brown sound” of Arrendendo-mod Super Lead Plexi

amps.name: ATOMICA LOW
body: Model: Cameron Atomica (low gain input)
Note: See 036  Atomica for more info

amps.name: BAND-COMMANDER
body: Model: ‘68 silverface Fender Bandmaster head with blackface AB763 circuit, Vibrato channel model
Orig. Cab: Fender 2x12
DynaCab: 2x12 65 Bassguy
Controls: Vol(Drive), Bright, Treble, Bass, Tremolo
Preamp tubes: 12AX7A (Syl)
Power tubes: 6L6/5881
Tonestack location: PRE
Notes: “Vibrato channel is loaded down by vibrato circuit, normal channel isn’t. Can probably get close via vibrato channel model increasing MV trim.”
AB763 Bandmaster known as holy grail Fender

“Blackface Bandmaster ideal for those looking for pure Fender clean sound, without making your ears bleed. Blackface & silverface Bandmaster is big sounding amp with flexible speaker impedance of 4ohm, allowing anything between 1 & 4 speakers (8ohm each) connected via main &/or external speaker jack, making it possible to adapt to small & big stages/gigs just by configuring speakers”
A lot of headroom. When it does start to break up, produces nice overdrive, overall sound is very balanced & doesn’t need much tweaking
No Middle tone control, keep at default for authenticity. No Master Volume either, so keep Master control cranked
Bandmaster offers 2 inputs per channel. Fractal models are always based on input with highest level. To get equivalent of lower input, set Input Trim to 0.500 in amp block
Tips: Screams “Play a John Mayer tune”
Sounds awesome at default settings

amps.name: BIG HAIR
body: Model: Custom FAS model, Sounds like ‘80s JCM 800
DynaCab: 4x12 1960TV
Power tubes: 6L6
Notes: Big Hair amp model sounds like JCM 800, just compare it to Brit 800 Mod amp model with low-gain T808 OD Drive block in front of it
Mids without mud, & revives ‘80’s metal scene
“I tuned it by ear, it was a total guess. It’s my idea of what 80’s hair metal might sound like.” -FAS

“FAS models usually remove/lower undesirable characteristics of old amps. Guitar tube amp design was nascent in 60s/70s & designers didn’t fully understand impact of overdriving tubes. One of biggest issues was blocking distortion, which is excessive bias excursion. When grid conducts, it shifts bias point & in those old amps, it can shift so much that tube goes into cutoff prematurely. More modern designs employ grid stoppers & smaller coupling capacitors to reduce bias excursion.”
Tips: For more ‘80’s revival, add detune Pitch block & circular delay after Cab block
Logical cab choice would be IR of 4x12 Marshall cab with G12M, V30 or G12-75 speakers

amps.name: BLANKENSHIP LEEDS
body: Model: Dweezil Zappa Blankenship Leeds 21
Orig. Cab: 2x10, 1x12, 2x12 (G12M)
DynaCab: 1x12 Div13 CJ11
Controls: Volume, Tone & tremolo
Preamp tubes: ECC83
Power tubes: EL84
Tonestack location: POST
Notes: 18w Marshall Mini Bluesbreaker replica, big sound, despite pretty low power “18w Marshalls kind of a bad design, phase inverter drives power tubes too hard, excessive bias excursion”
Tone mapped to Treble in Amp block, no hi-cut/ Depth, controls don’t do anything
“Phase inverter has significant bias excursion, causes amp to sound more open when driven hard (at expense of crossover distortion)”
Tips: “Max out Volume (Input Drive), turn up Tone (Treble) & use guitar’s volume knob to regulate gain.”
Marshall RI has 20w G12M speaker. Leeds 21 has 2 G12Hs. Pick from Marshall stock cabs, or try IR of a smaller cab/speaker, like Triptik, Nuclear Tone, Scumtone or Division 13

amps.name: BLUDOJAI CLEAN
body: Model: Bludotone ECC83, clean channel, PAB (Preamp Bypass off)
Orig. Cab: Bludotone 1x12, 2x12 (G12-65, EVM)
DynaCab: 4x12 Rumble EV12L
Controls: Volume Treble, Middle, Bass, Master, Presence, Bright. Switchable PAB: bypass tonestack (Bass/Mid/Treble)
Bright: add sparkle, less obvious if Vol is up
Mid: boosts mids for more gain, like FAT switch
Rock/Jazz: Jazz setting applies LP filtering
On real amp, 1st 2 switches often off & often set to Rock, models were created like that
Preamp tubes: 12AX7A (Syl)
Power tubes: 6L6
Tonestack location: MID
Notes: “Same schematic as Robben Ford’s famous “Tan” Dumble OD Special amp

Bright cap on Master Volume, turning MV down, gets thinner, makes MV even more sensitive than usual, as there’s another interaction going on. To get most range from amp, experiment with MV, listen interaction with low/high Drive settings, like real amp. Breakup range/tones between is wide, dynamic & responds to playing dynamics, getting crunchier digging in & cleaner playing lighter
Bass response increase if MV up from default
Famous Dumble players: Larry Carlton, John Mayer, Santana, Keith Urban, SRV, Eric Johnson, Bonamassa
“Real amp has OD Vol (AKA Ratio) & MV, assume max OD Vol”

“Pre, mid & post aren’t absolute locations, relative to model. In Clean model, there’s only buffer & 1 gain stage, mid means stack is between buffer & gain stage. Changing to pre puts it before buffer. Since there’s only 2 tubes, pre would indicate before buffer, which isn’t what we want. In PAB model there is no tone stack so I put a neutral tone stack after the gain stages (with a fixed attenuation after the buffer). This allows the most flexibility. The HRM case the neutral tone stack is replaced by a Marshall tone stack.”
More to Dumble than Robben Ford’s tone. Each Dumble was voiced specific to its buyer. Replicating Dumbles is hard, amp circuit often has goop all over it, hiding electronics

Robben Ford’s Dumble tone is an elusive tone, worshiped by jazz/blues/fusion players. Thick, mid-heavy, smooth & very touch-sensitive lead tones. Trademark tone isn’t for everybody: hit power chord & it may be terrible to your ears. “Note flip” is a certain tonal characteristic, attributed to Dumble & some clones. Note flip? “It sounds KIND of like it’s starting to feed back, but it’s not. It flips note up, so a different harmonic order is heard, or something like that”.

“Ojai is exact copy of Dumble #102. It requires Dumbleator & specific capacitance cables to run to & from Dumbleator (about 180-200pf per cable), due to large bright cap on amp master. All controls marked in silver sharpie for exact settings of Robbens tone. Pot tapers are all 30% audio taper, except treble control, presence & OD in/outs, which are linear. Robben sets mids at ~70K from ground.” –The Gear Page
Tips: Pairs well with factory Rumble 4X12 cab
Pair with G12-65H speaker. BludoMix Cab in factory firmware is 1x12 Alnico dual port cab & will sound good, but perhaps a tad darker (great for Jazz) on clean sounds than G12-65H will
Put a Zen drive in front of BludoClean amp, or a boost & listen to how that clean channel wakes up for grittier leads & touch-responsiveness

Master Volume amp, so amp’s distortion created by preamp tubes, not in power amp. MV works in power amp section, important to tone & feel. Decide what setting in amp model works best, default is good start point. MV has bright cap on it: turned down, gets brighter to compensate

amps.name: BLUDOJAI LEAD
body: Model: Bludotone Ojai, OD channel, PAB off
Controls: Clean model controls+OD Drive/level
Tonestack location: POST
Notes: Easy model for singing Dumble lead tone

“Pre, mid & post aren’t absolute locations, they’re relative to particular model. In Lead model, there’s a buffer & then 3 gain stages IIRC. Pre means tone stack is before 3 gain stage here. Changing it to Mid put it after 1st gain stage”
Note: See 041  Bludojai for more info
Tips: Default values, set MV 5, Drive & Overdrive 4-5 & experiment with tone stack swaps

If wanting amp to feedback effortlessly on note, raise overdrive setting higher combined with gain, given you have sufficient volume coming out of speakers to hit guitar pickups in a reinforcing loop, it will do it! Throw a boost or Zen drive on that, if you like...”

amps.name: BLUDOJAI LEAD PAB
body: Model: Bludotone Ojai, OD channel, PAB on
Controls: Clean controls+OD Drive/Level
Tonestack location: PRE
Notes: See 041 & 042 Bludojai for more info
Easy model for singing Dumble lead tone
Neutral tone stack, same as lifting ground. All knobs at 50% for authenticity. Unlike amp, adjust tone controls & something happens”

“Pre, mid & post aren’t absolute locations, they’re relative to particular model. In PAB model, there’s no tone stack, so I put neutral tone stack after gain stages (with fixed attenuation after buffer), for most flexibility”

amps.name: BOGFISH BROWN
body: Model: Bogner Fish preamp (Brown channel)
Brown channel has the most gain
DynaCab: 4x12 Friedman V30
Controls: Volume(Drive), Treble, Middle, Bass, MV
Preamp tubes: ECC83
Power tubes: EL34
Tonestack location: POST
Notes: “I used the power amp models from the XTC.”
The Fish is an all-tube preamp from ’90s, famous for use by Jerry Cantrell, of Alice in Chains

“The Fish preamp was created in ‘89-90 & ushered in a new era of boutique design & tone. Featured 4 channels: Country, Strato, Shark & Brown, covered a wide range of tones, from crystalline clean to heavy modified amps tones, all within a 2-space rack mountable chassis.” – Bogner
“I was a Bogner man, I owned various Bogner Ecstasy models. It was my mainstay amp before I got Axe-Fx Ultra. Not as thick as trademark Bogner tones, less depth, slightly papery.” – yek

amps.name: BOGFISH STRATO
body: Model: Bogner Fish preamp (Strato channel)
Note: See 044  Bogfish for more info

amps.name: BRIT 800 2204 HIGH
body: Model: 50W Marshall JCM800 2204, High sensitivity in
Orig. Cab: Marshall 4x12 (G12M, G12H, G12-75, V30)
DynaCab: 4x12 1960TV
Controls: Preamp Vol, Bass, Mid, Treble, Presence, MV
Preamp tubes: ECC83
Power tubes: EL34/6550
Tonestack location: POST
Notes: JCM has always been associated with hard rock in the ‘80s & 90s, used by Slash, Iron Maiden, Slayer, etc

“Very bright model, but sounds just like amp modeled. If too bright, adjust various tone controls to reduce brightness to tastes. Designed to be loud & brightness decreases as MV increases. Sound of 80’s hair metal”
“Saturation switch switches a zener diode clipping stage right before tone stack (Arrendondo Mod)”
“Rips my head off here. I have to turn presence way down. Check MV. Too high & gets muddy.”

“JCM800s sound better with hot bias IMO. Problem is plate voltage is too high, so when setting bias higher, dissipation goes up & tube life is shortened. IMO they should’ve made plate voltage around 420V, would allow hotter bias with longer tube life, at expense of little less volume, but JCM800s are ungodly loud anyways, so...“

amps.name: BRIT 800 2204 LOW
body: Model: 50w Marshall JCM800, 2204, low sensitivity input
Note: See 046  Brit 800 for more info

amps.name: BRIT 800 2203 HIGH
body: Model: 100W Marshall JCM 800 2203 JMP (high input)
Orig. Cab: Marshall 4x12 (G12M)
DynaCab: 4x12 1960TV
Controls: Presence, Bass, Middle, Treble, MV, Preamp Vol
Preamp tubes: ECC83
Power tubes: EL34/6550
Notes: Unmodified, vertical inputs & drake transformers g2204 models have 10K dropping resistor between B+ & PI. 2203 have 20K, about 17% more preamp gain vs 2204

“Reference amp received had no bright cap. It wasn’t snipped out. It may have been removed by unsoldering it, but if they did, they cleaned up as there’s no residual flux on terminals. 2nd JCM800 I’ve encountered with no bright cap. I installed bright cap, because every schematic shows a bright cap & model based on amp w/ bright cap installed

Other info: B+ bit on low side, ~450V. Feedback is 100K from 4-ohm tap. Transformers same as US/UK versions. OT has 4/8/16 taps, but 16-ohm tap isn’t connected. Preamp measures virtually identical to my 2204”
“The 2204 models have a 10K dropping resistor between the B+ & PI. The 2203 models have 20K. The 2203 has about 17% more preamp gain than the 2204 as a result”
See 046  Brit 800 for more info
Tips: Preamp gain is very low in these amps. Turn Master Volume up. I usually run it at 10

amps.name: BRIT 800 2203 LOW
body: Model: 100W Marshall JCM 800 2203 JMP (low input)
Notes: See 046  Brit 800 for more info

amps.name: BRIT 800 #34
body: Model: 50W Marshall JCM 800, Santiago #34 modifications
DynaCab: 4x12 1960TV
Power tubes: EL34/6550
Notes: Santiago #34 mods, makes it sound like rental amp #34, from company S.I.R., which was used on parts on Appetite for Destruction album

“Brit 800 #34 does better Slash than AFS100 IMO. AFS100 models are based on actual amp, but I think my version of #34 sounds more like AFD. I based model on info I collected over the years about mods done.”

“Has ton of treble boost & squeals with some guitars, IRs &/or types of amplification. Nature of the beast. No other amp has near amount of treble boost. AFD100 Marshall produced later (#34 copy) has nowhere near the treble boost & huge snubber cap on PI (rolls off treble). Gut tells me they did this, as they feared there’d be too many reports of pickup squeal (or amp too unstable at high gain)”
See 046  Brit 800 for more info

amps.name: BRIT 800 MOD
body: Model: 50W Marshall JCM 800 with mods
DynaCab: 4x12 1960TV
Preamp tubes: ECC83
Power tubes: EL34/6550
Notes: JCM800 with mods, sounds heavier & less strident
“Has extra gain stage & 1uF bypass cap on last triode.”
See 046  Brit 800 for more info

amps.name: BRIT 800 STUDIO 20
body: Model: Marshall Studio Classic SC20H
DynaCab: 4x12 1960TV
Controls: Presence, Bass, Middle, Treble, Master, Preamp Vol
Preamp tubes: ECC83
Power tubes: EL34/6550
Notes: “That little JCM800 Studio is a cool amp, doesn’t sound like its big brothers, but has its own thing going on that’s interesting. Sorta like Marshall meets Vox.”
See 046  Brit 800 for more info

amps.name: BRIT AFS100 1
body: Model: Marshall Slash AFD100 Sign. Special Collector Ed. #76
Orig. Cab: Marshall 4x12 (V30)
DynaCab: 4x12 Friedman V30
Controls: Gain (Drive), Bass, Middle, Treble, Presence, Master
Preamp tubes: ECC83
Power tubes: 6550/EL34
Tonestack location: POST
Notes: Modded JCM 800, model 2203, rental amp #34 from S.I.R., used on parts on Appetite for Destruction. “I have Slash signature amp model, spent a lot of time measuring, listening & comparing”
“Brit 800 #34 does better Slash than AFS100 IMO, based on actual amp, but I think my version of #34 sounds more like AFD. I based model on info collected over years about mods done.”

“Amount of treble peaking needed for amp at a given drive is dependent on the guitar (guess is AFD100 designed with a Les Paul. With my Suhr, it’s too bright, as it’s a very bright guitar. One thing I do with AFS100 is turn down bright cap a bit.”
“Default MV quite high, masks Presence control effect, as power amp is distorting heavily. Amp sounds best if power amp’s driven hard. Try turning MV down & notice Presence has more effect”
Tips: Has MV, amp’s distortion created by preamp/power tubes. Don’t hesitate to turn it up.

According to manual, Slash sets all amp controls ~1 o’clock. Works in FAS model too. Presence/MV default, use Les Paul & remember: while Axe gain/tone controls visually match controls of original amps (within 10%), MV/Presence/Depth don’t
Slash uses V30s in his Marshall cabs

amps.name: BRIT AFS100 2
body: Model: Marshall Slash AFD100 AFD mode
Notes: Based on Super Lead Plexi, supposedly, which modded to add gain & master volume. Thicker & more gain than mode #34. Responsible for THE sound that defines Appetite For Destruction.

Story goes original amp was of almost mystical proportions. Modded & sounding so good that everybody wanted to rent it. Loved so much by Slash that he falsely reported it as stolen. It was reclaimed later by the rental company, but gone missing now. Manual says it was a modded Super Lead Tremolo 1959T, rental #39. But, according to report, Slash didn’t use rental amp #39 at all. He used a substitute model 1959 (#36), with same mod as #39
AFD modeled by Fractal (Brit Super amp model). Unlike AFS100 2 model, Brit Super was based on schematic only & therefore sounds different vs this model (more gain & boosted high end)
See 046  Brit 800 for more info

amps.name: BRIT BROWN
body: Model: Custom FAS model Plexi “Brown Sound”
DynaCab: 4x12 5153
Power tubes: EL34
Notes: “Brit Brown was built by ear”
Fractal’s take on pure, raw guitar tones of early Van Halen albums
“FAS take on how ultimate “Brown” plexi should sound like, based on 100W SLP with Arredondo mods & few little voicing tweaks”
Marshall Super Lead Plexi, perhaps modded, with Variac to run at lower than normal voltage, 4x12 cab with JBLs/greenbacks, copious (panned) reverb & some EQ magic at mixing table

Supposedly Eddie himself said: “There’s a difference between being just loud & having what I call a warm, brown sound, a toney sound.” Another story tells term “brown sound” originally used to describe Alex Van Halen’s drum sound. Legendary Tones
Saturation on simulates Jose Arrendendo mod (has aggressive tone)

“FAS models remove/lower undesirable character of old amps. Tube amp design starting in 60/70s, designers didn’t understand full OD tube impact. Big issue was blocking distortion, excessive bias excursion. When grid conducts, it shifts bias point, in old amps, can shift so much tube goes into cutoff prematurely. Modern designs has grid stoppers & smaller coupling caps to reduce bias excursion” g0.68uF cap on 2nd triode
Tips: Sound on early Van Halen records is immediately recognizable. If you listen to isolated Van Halen guitar tracks, you’ll hear that early EVH didn’t use that much gain & his tone was very bright
Can be a little dull at default. Increasing Presence solves that.

Eddie used to crank all amp controls. That sound is already modeled, don’t have to do that, but there’s no rules. Use Marshall cabs & G12Ms/JBLs for authenticity. Didn’t use V30s/custom speakers until later

amps.name: BRIT JM45
body: Model: 30w Marshall JTM 45
Orig. Cab: Marshall 4x12 (G12M, G12H)
DynaCab: 4x12 1960TV
Controls: Volume Normal/High Treble, Bass, Mid, Treble, Presence
Preamp tubes: ECC83
Power tubes: EL34/6L6/KT66
Tonestack location: POST

amps.name: BRIT JM45 JUMPED
body: Tips: If wanting Channel 1 sound, use Brit JM45 Jump model & turn Treble Drive knob all way down
Turn up mids to decrease scooped character
Some use patch cables to jumper inputs: 2nd input of channel 1 goes to 1st input of channel 2, enables having benefits of both channels at once
Model has 2 separate Drive controls, set at same position, or keep Normal Drive lower than Treble Drive

amps.name: BRIT JVM OD1 GREEN
body: Model: Marshall JVM410H, Green OD1 Channel
Orig. Cab: Marshall 4x12 G12T75
DynaCab: 4x12 1960TV
Controls: MV, Bass, Middle, Treble, Gain (Drive), Presence, Resonance (Depth)
Preamp tubes: ECC83
Power tubes: EL34
Tonestack location: POST
Notes: Green OD1 channel similar to hot-rodded JCM 800

JVM410H is Marshall’s flagship guitar amp, a 100 watt EL34- powered head with 4 channels & 3 modes (voices) per channel. Its 12 voices cover almost Marshall’s entire sound spectrum: JTM 45, Plexi, JCM 800 etc, & add ultra-high gain

“IMO, reason JVM sounds good is plate cap on 2nd-to-last triode, smooths out tone considerably. 2nd-to-last triode associated with Triode 1 Freq in advanced parameters, can adjust to simulate adding cap to plate. Other thing helping tone is 220K plate resistor on last triode, shifts bias point down vs classic Marshall. Unfortunately bias points not exposed to user”
Power amp based on JCM 800 2203 & 59 Super Lead Plexi

British-built, 100 Watt all-valve JVM410H head redefines versatility thanks to 4 tonally independent channels: Clean, Crunch, OD1 & OD2, each with 3 switchable modes. Each mode reconfigures gain structure of each, for 12 modes total

JVM410H front panel looks mind boggling with 28 controls & 8 switches, but as it’s intuitively laid out, it’s real simple to use. Also features 4 studio-quality digital Reverbs (1 per channel), individual channel EQ, 2 MV & memory to recall settings
Combining some of the finest Marshall sounds, has vast tonal palette, from ‘Plexi’/JTM45 cleans through JCM800 roar, to modern high gain.” – Marshall
Tips: Models have lot of gain, keep Input Drive low

amps.name: BRIT JVM OD1 ORANGE
body: Notes: OD1 Orange: additional gain stage, for hard rock/leads
Models have lot of gain, keep Input Drive low

amps.name: BRIT JVM OD1 RED
body: Notes: OD1 Red: adds more gain
Models have lot of gain, keep Input Drive low

amps.name: BRIT JVM OD2 GREEN
body: Notes: Green OD2: like hot-rodded JCM 800 with even more gain & Middle control shifted down, centered around 500Hz, instead of more typical Marshall value of 650Hz
Model has so much gain, suffices to keep Input Drive pretty low

amps.name: BRIT JVM OD2 ORANGE
body: Notes: OD2 Orange: additional gain stage, for hard rock/leads, with even more gain & Middle control shifted down, centered around 500Hz, instead of more typical Marshall value of 650Hz
Model have so much gain it suffices to keep Input Drive pretty low

amps.name: BRIT JVM OD2 RED
body: Notes: OD2 Red: with even more gain & Middle control shifted down, centered around 500Hz, instead of more typical Marshall value of 650Hz
Model have so much gain it suffices to keep Input Drive pretty low

amps.name: BRIT PRE
body: Model: Marshall JMP-1 preamp (OD Channel)
Controls: Volume, Gain, Bass, Middle, Treble, Presence
Preamp tubes: ECC83
Tonestack location: POST
Notes: MIDI-controllable rack-mount tube preamp, based on JCM 900, Marshall’s amp from ‘90s. Bands like Def Leppard, ZZ Top & Deftones known for using JMP-1
Don’t confuse this preamp with current JMP1 “baby” Marshall
Fractal’s 1st model of JMP-1 was Brit Pre, model was reworked, resulting in JMPre-1 models. Brit Pre amp model stayed on because it’s used by several prominent “A-list” artists
Power amp section based on ‘90s Marshall 100w power amp
JMP-1 preamp has 4 modes: 2 Clean ones, OD1 & OD2. Fractal modeled both OD modes.

OD1: “warm vintage drive reminiscent of Marshall Super Lead amps through to modern day MVs”. & “features purity of distortion from golden age of plexi glass Super Leads. As gain increased, passes through degrees of distortion into rich, higher gain mode, with right nuances for modern blues rock player. Vintage crunch, modern metal & fat blues lead sounds all readily available.” – Marshall

OD2 is “tightly focused high gain drive, can be smooth or aggressive depending on EQ & gain settings”. & “Tightly focused stunning high gain is what you find in OD2. Modern & aggressive, smooth & fluid, gain levels & singing tone of channel give infinite sustain, while retaining pure toneful quality.” – Marshall
No Master Volume, as it’s a preamp

“2 channels have preset tone stacks, then there’s a digitally controlled 4-band graphic EQ, which is B/M/T & Presence. This technique gets around problem of trying to digitally control a tone stack. Triaxis uses LDRs in place of potentiometers. The Soldano X-99 uses motorized pots. Both techniques being expensive. So JMP-1 uses fixed tone stack & then digital-controlled graphic EQ. Axe-Fx model of the JMP-1 is “better” in sense that tone controls control tone stack, rather than graphic EQ. Then have separate graphic EQ to further tailor sound. What I didn’t do & why people probably feel model differs, is “normalized” tone controls, so with B/M/T at noon, tone stacks match. On Axe-Fx may need to set Bass to 3, Mid to 7, etc. to get tone to match. I just used standard Marshall tone stack, whereas JMP-1 uses standard Marshall tone stack, but pots are replaced with fixed resistors, but values don’t necessarily correspond to pots at noon.”

“Man, that Brit Pre is just a magic amp model to my ears. I know some celebrity guitar players who played JMPs for years & they prefer it. Speaking of Def Lep, I sat & dialed in Axe-Fx presets using Phil Collen’s actual JMP rig, we chose Brit Pre” - Admin M@
Tips: Visual tone control settings don’t line up to actual amp due to different tone stack

“Models of JMP-1 are well balanced. At default values, already sound fine, although may want to turn up gain. I prefer non- BS models, as I don’t like too much bass in my tone, but it’s a matter of personal preference” –yek
JMP-1 manual Settings (tone settings may not match AxeFx Tone controls):

amps.name: BRIT SILVER
body: Model: Marshall Silver Jubilee
(Lead channel, Gain in)
Orig Cab: Marshall 4x12 V30
DynaCab: 4x12 Lerxst
Controls: Presence, Bass, Mid, Treble, Gain, Master
Preamp tubes: ECC83
Power tubes: EL34
Tonestack location: POST
Notes: “Model is now based on a Silver Jubilee, mid pot is log. Guess Alex likes mids... So if you want the sound of the old model, turn up the mids.”
“Lead channel w/ gain knob pushed in. Pull rhythm clip thing in this amp is an enigma. It implies it’s only active in rhythm channel, but it affects lead channel too.”
Darker/thicker vs JCM, more gain at lower levels, even approaches Dumble-territory
Jubilee players: Joe Bonamassa, Alex Lifeson (Rush), Slash used one in his live GnR rig & playing with Velvet Revolver, prior to Marshall AFD100, his signature was a Jubilee
“Looking at Marshall’s current catalogue, I’d much rather own a 2555 re-issue (Jubilee) than JVM410. 2555 reminds me of amps by Bogner, Friedman & Carol-Ann.” – yek

With my Suhr it’s much too bright because that’s a very bright guitar. 1st thing I do with AFS100 model is turn down bright cap a bit.”
“I have actual Slash signature model & spent lot of time measuring, listening & comparing.”
“Brit 800 #34 does better Slash than AFS100 IMO, based on actual amp, but I think my version of #34 sounds more like AFD. I based model on info collected about mods done.”

“Amount of treble peaking needed for amp at a given drive is highly dependent on guitar. Ex: my guess is AFD100 was designed around Les Paul. With my Suhr, it’s much too bright, as it’s a very bright guitar. So one of 1st things I do with AFS100 model is turn down bright cap a bit.”

“Default MV is quite high in model, which masks effect of Presence control, since power amp’s distorting heavily. Find amp sounds best with power amp driven hard). Turn MV down & notice Presence control has much more effect.”

Tips: According to amp’s manual, Slash sets all controls on his amp around 1 o’clock, works in FAS model too, Presence/MV at default. Use Les Paul & remember: while gain & tone controls in amp models visually match controls on original amps (within 10%), Master/Presence/Depth controls don’t
Has MV, amp’s distortion created by pre/power tubes. Turn it up!
Slash uses V30s in his Marshall cabs

I’m convinced feedback is connected to wrong node but I quadruple-checked & my schematic is definitely correct. I think it was probably a mistake but they said “Hmm, but it sounds good so don’t fix it”
“Amp’s midrange push gives Strats a certain weightiness that give its normally weak bridge pickup some added heft. To boot, even single notes from Strats seem to carry more authority

A Les Paul into high-gain input takes you straight to Rock City, creating creamy, robust distorted tones that clean up nicely for chunky rhythm riffs (by dialing it down to about 7 on guitar’s Volume knob). Switching back to the low-gain input cleans things up, but the Twinmaster could never be considered a clean machine when using a humbucker-equipped guitar like the ’59.” - Vintage Guitar

Have probably heard it on many albums, was a common studio device. Famous for clean tone (like blackface Fender) & its crunch & lead OD tones. #3 in name denotes # of channels
CA3+ users: Steve Lukather, Peter Frampton, EVH (F.U.C.K. album), Billie Joe Armstrong (Green Day), Metallica, Megadeth
CAE also builds OD-100 amp head, based on the 3+SE preamp. John Suhr, who worked at CAE/CAA & 3+SE & OD-100 co- designer, started his own company, sells OD-100 under CAA name.
“Clean channel is extremely warm & punchy with tons of tight low end, smooth midrange & silky highs.

Tips: “James tonestack, typically you’d get close via shelving filters with freqs of 100/1000Hz”

amps.name: CAMERON CCV 1A
body: Model: Cameron CCV-100, Channel 1, less gain

Controls: Channel 1: Punch (Resonance/Depth), Presence, Master, Bass, Middle, Treble & Gain, 3-way Voicing switch (resonance), 3-way Dark switch (presence), 3-way Gain Style switch (Jose Master gain/clipping), 3-way Bright switch (less noticeable at higher gain settings)
Preamp tubes: 12AX7A (JJ)
Power tubes: EL34
Tonestack location: PRE
Notes: “Quirky amp, kind of a 1-trick pony but what a trick”
(Drive knob) “ref amp has linear taper pot, so behavior’s very abrupt. Matches amp extremely accurately”
“Not a very high gain amp. Topology similar to JCM800”
“Key to CCV sound is Sat Switch”
“Camerons have linear taper MV & needs it, as signal level into power amp is very low, due to Jose topology”

“Humans perceive high freqs thermal noise as hiss. More gain, more hiss. Diezel’s have lot of filtering. Large grid stoppers, plate capacitors, etc, reducing bandwidth which lowers hiss. Camerons are pretty wide open. Small grid stoppers or bypass caps on grid stoppers, no plate caps, etc”
“Presence cap is very large compared to typical Plexi. Therefore, presence control lets lot more mids through when turned up.”

CCV doesn’t have that much gain. It just explodes because of engaged Saturation parameter, attributes to its mean sound. It sounds nastier (& much brighter) than Cameron Atomica, a side-product of these cutting tones is you may not like them as much for single notes high on neck, can sound a little thin
Gain (Drive) taper control is peculiar, goes from 0-100 at start, then hardly changes for remainder

Amp with MV on both channels, distortion created by preamp tubes, not power amp. MV works in power amp section, still very important to tone & feel. Have to decide for yourself what setting in amp model works best. Default setting is a good point to start
Tips: Dark switch is Negative feedback control. Set Negative Feedback to 3.6 to reproduce switch in middle position. Set to 9.8 to reproduce switch in right position. 5.0 for left position (default)

“When adding gain (via gain/clipping switches), turn back gain pots for more noticeable effect of gain switches (for better cut & more articulation). Can roll back to noon & amp still has lotta gain. There’s no right or wrong way to set it. If it sounds good, it is good” g4x12 cab with Celestion G12H speakers
Designed to be used with 4x12 cabs with G12H speakers. Find list of stock G12H cabs in the wiki. Alternatively, use 5153 stock cabs
“Bright-1 switch selects Bright cap, can alter via Advanced menu”
“Has lotta mids already, don’t hesitate to turn Mid down” - manual

amps.name: CAR AMBLER
body: Model: Carr Rambler
Orig. Cab: Carr 1x12 (Eminence Elsinore)
DynaCab: 1x12 Car Ambler
Controls: Volume, Treble, Middle, Bass, Tremolo effect
Preamp tubes: 12AX7A (Syl)
Power tubes: 6L6
Tonestack location:
Power tubes: 6L6
Tonestack location: PRE
Notes: Modern take on Fender Deluxe Reverb, with extra chime, presence & headroom. Fender’s headroom is limited, Rambler has ample.
“Basically Deluxe Reverb preamp with cathode bias 6L6 power amp & no negative feedback. Sorta Fender-meets- Vox thing.”
On TGP & similar boards, Rambler gets lot of praise for pristine clean tones & does sound great g28w, single channel, cathode bias combo. Switchable between Pentode (28w) & ½-power Triode (14w)
“Rambler employs pure & simple, classic 60s American preamp circuit with output section, owing more to early 50s. Unique blending yields very open & rich clean tones with mild OD.”

(About comparing Rambler to Fender Deluxe) “Both amps occupy same place in general range of amps out there, it’s easy to see why people compare them, but Rambler & Deluxe behave very differently in practical use. At 28w, it makes more power & cleaner than Deluxe & their preamp sections are similar, but Rambler’s power section is much more like Tweed Pro & phase inverter is based on a Princeton. So, the Rambler shares heritage with a few Fender classics.” – Carr

“Rambler was 1 of my earliest signature designs & today it’s still 1 of our most popular models. Why? It’s no accident that Rambler succeeds in delivering classic American deluxe tone, expanded clean headroom, solid bass response & option of 28w pentode or 14w triode operation. Rambler simply reflects my desire to improve & refine the original concept of an appropriately powered 1x12 club amp with reverb & tremolo. Rambler owners describe their amps as being warm, full & round, with surprisingly high threshold of clean tone (also makes it uniquely suited to virtually any OD pedal). Rambler is considered by many to be quintessential club amp, voiced to meet needs of virtually any player & all types of music.”

“Enter one of my absolute favorite real amps ever. I’ve used a Carr Rambler as one of my main amps for >10 years & continue to absolutely love it! Has all qualities that makes a vintage Deluxe Reverb great (current RI Deluxe Reverbs sound nothing like real vintage one IMO), with bit more punch, warmth & oomph provided by 6L6 based power amp section, plus it gets close to tweed sound when dialed in that way, push mids & experiment with pentode vs triode mode. Tube based tremolo & reverb both really wonderful. Also, Rambler takes pedals like no other amp I’ve tried & could easily be my only amp. Rambler ties with Reinau Modern Tweed Deluxe & Tweed Twin as my fav clean real amps of all time & Axe version of Rambler is really great & sounds just as good as the real thing when matched with a great IR.” – Rocket Brother
Can get amp to distort, but isn’t unique selling point of amp
Tips: Rambler is combo amp in various configs: 1x12, 2x12, 2x10 or 1x15, with Eminence speakers
There’s 2 Roamer stock cabs
Alternatively, try Fender stock cab
Beware of bass: has a lot, especially with mids turned up

Pentode: punchy, full sound / Triode: mellow & thick

amps.name: CAROL-ANN OD-2
body: Model: Carol-Ann OD-2
Orig. Cab: EV-12L, Classic Lead 80, Scholz
DynaCab: 1x12 Scholz
Controls: MV, PreGain, Drive
Bass, Mid, Treble, Presence
Preamp tubes: 12AX7A (Syl)
Power tubes: 6L6
Tonestack location: POST
Notes: “CAs are definitely dark amps. Sound great though”
Like Dumble sound with more gain, w/o fuzzy Fuchs? Try Carol-Anns
Sorta Dumble-style amp with character of a modified Marshall

Though power amp/preamp are modeled, preamp got most effort. As said in another thread, I tuned by putting Axe into actual power amp of OD2 & that side by side with full OD2. Final tweaks made using parametric EQ blocks hard coded into model, call it the icing on the cake & basically same level of tuning into model as with actual OD2, difference is I used a GUI, not resistors & capacitors.”

“OD2 & to degree, OD3 uses HRM style gain staging with Plexi PI & power amp. Really like idea of driving tonestack from common cathode stage, as opposed to cathode follower like Marshall, as it got rid of some of artifacts I don’t like, with say JCM-800. That’s something Dumble came up with I have to give major props for. I wanted an amp that’d have little more gain vs JCM-800, but with character of Plexi, without fizz or harsh upper mid/lower treble freqs. Remember this was BEFORE anyone had even degooped or documented Bluesmaster style circuit & only thing Shad was building was his post count at AmpGarage, asking about Dumble circuits. I find it amazing it took guys years to catch on with moving pots to front. Does require bit more thought than just moving pots as ranges of those trimmers Dumble used is impractical when used as front panel controls (they weren’t really intended to be). Do also agree that from pure tone standpoint, nothing on front panel is always gonna be better. JB100 model’s basically an OD2 with entire OD circuit on the board, with predefined filters made up of quality components, without adjustment through fragile trimmers. OD2 was never designed to be put side by side in a Dumble clone fest, in some guys living room with everyone playing their best Ford licks, it was designed to be used on stage, for guys playing in cover/blues/wedding/classic rock bands. Separate EQ & tube driven effects loop were added for that situation. I’ve always tried to avoid that association as much as possible, as average Dumble lover is typically looking for a particular set of tones that may/may not be found in this amp. Early customer interaction made me wanna ensure people knew it wasn’t a tonal clone of Dumble, to avoid misunderstandings. OD-2 isn’t best amp for those tones, I agree, but Dumble or clone doesn’t really do fast dampened power chords like OD-2 does either, it’s much more suited for blues & classic rock, not Ford/Carlton thing. Bear in mind, average player’s looking for function/tone, not particular design architecture. Fact I used HRM style circuit obviously (& unfortunately) placed me in Dumble category. But 2500 amps later, not really seen that way anymore, as CA’s always had their own thing going on tonally, much more in a modded Marshall vein. Newer models, like Triptik 2, have nothing in common with any Dumble, not even cathode driven tonestack. For anyone that’s played real Dumbles, there’s a certain thing (heard more in clean channel) that’s uniquely his signature, certain swirl to clean tone always in his amps. However, amp doesn’t OD anywhere near what I like when pushed hard. For me, I’d rather focus on that tone in a Plexi. Who knows what original guy he modded this for wanted. Hopefully this explains why I always shied away from Dumble comparison, never trying to clone a Dumble.” - CA

“Not everyone falls into hatred of tube amps & despising guys like me, who designs things you guys wanna emulate, because they own an AxeFX. While there may be childish ‘them & us’ on forums, there isn’t at a pro level. Most accomplished players don’t fall in strict category & use both if needed. What better way to get amp in front of 1000s of amateur & pro players, than ensure model’s close as possible, beats clips online. AxeFX is a tool, so is a tube amp, they’re not political statements, they don’t even compete. Bottom line, it helps sell amps! Probably pisses some closed minded idiots off, but they win in next free software update with another no-cost, new amp.

amps.name: CAROL-ANN TRIPTIK CLASSIC
body: Model: Carol-Ann Triptik 50w, Classic Channel
Orig. Cab: Carol-Ann 1x12 (Scholz Classic)
DynaCab: 1x12 Scholz
Controls: Input Level, Drive (channels 2&3), Bass, Mid, Treble, Presence, Master, Classic/Modern voice switch
Preamp tubes: 12AX7A
Power tubes: EL34
Tonestack location: POST
Notes: “TripTik is badass. Dumble meets Marshall with sprinkle of 5150 power amp.”
“I think what people like about this amp is same reason people like the BE/HBE, share same aggressive low-cut on input, then add bass back in power amp for clear bass response w/o flub”
“Some amps have fixed depth circuit, like 5153, Freidman BE/HBE, Dirty Shirley, TripTik, Tucana, etc. With these, Depth knob default value corresponds to fixed circuit”
“CAs are definitely dark amps. Sound great though”
“If you like base Dumble sound, but want little more gain without fuzziness of Fuchs, try Carol-Ann models”
“Does classic rock extremely well & one of best clean channels I’ve ever designed.” - Alan Phillips of Carol-Ann
Triptik is 50w, 3-channel amp with EL34 power tubes, designed for rock. Channel 1 handles clean sounds. 2nd & 3rd channels identical; Classic/Modern voicing switches sets their character.
Classic has little less gain, low end & produces 70s/80s British rock tones, wide/complex sound stage, no buzz/brittle highs
All channel Tone controls are effective & center points of each is perfect for exact freq bands that’ll push amp through mix

“Our idea for true rock amp for both timeless classic rock tones & modern high gain tones, with super tight & crushing low end. True ground up design & not inspired by any other amp. Unique gain structure doesn’t use high #s of cascading gain stages compressing life out of tone, nor use any solid state clipping. Amp OD generated by running each gain to absolute extreme, just keeping them this side of instability. Helps preserve dynamic range, noise to minimum, emphasize harmonic overtones of each note, not sounding bright/harsh”
Concept was produce amp that reproduces my fav mod rock amp tones & modern higher gain tones without mush/noise
As with all our amps, built by hand, has non-PCB main audio board, hard-wired chassis mounted controls & tube sockets”

“In Triptik, initial bass cut happens after 1st gain stage, incrementally brought back in through next stages, while also controlling & manipulating high & mid bands too. There’s several filters at various points in gain stages to manipulate these other bands. 1 band’s always relative to other 2. In other words, wanting more bass, don’t necessarily have to increase that, can attenuate other 2 bands to create same effect. Keeping bass out of earlier stages allows each stage to be set up for more gain, for more gain from less stages, keeps S/N ratio down & dynamic range up. Feedback loop in power amp is freq dependent & allows more mid-highs to pass back to phase inverter on inverted input out-of-phase with normal input, which flattens response & lowers power amp gain (closed loop gain) much more for just those 2 bands. Bass band not attenuated near as much so amplified at level closer to open loop gain of power amp.” – Alan Phillips
“I think what people like about this amp is same reason people like BE/HBE. They share same aggressive input low-cut, then add bass back in power amp, for clear bass response w/o flub”
Master Vol amp, distortion created by preamp tubes, not power amp. MV works in power amp & still important to tone/feel
Tips: Dark amp, don’t hesitate to increase Treble or Presence
(Classic mode) “On real amp, you’d set Input level 2 o’clock, Gain 1-2 o’clock, for great classic rock rhythm tone. Not overly different in character to modern setting, less gain & low end”
Try 1x12 Triptik IR stock cab. It’s special as it’s a boutique Scholz Classic (Sugar Cone) speaker, but pairs well with many
“I ran into combination of cabs that I just love with Triptik models: cab 54 (4x12 Pre-Rola 55) & 116 (Scumtone).” – yek

amps.name: CAROL-ANN TRIPTIK CLEAN
body: Model: Carol-Ann Triptik 50w, Clean Channel
Notes: Clean Channel has shimmer never heard with this tube type & incredible headroom. Pushing pre-gain control, can get slightly broken clean tones too. Takes OD pedals well
See 085 Carol-Ann Triptik for more info

See 085 Carol-Ann Triptik for more info

amps.name: CAROL-ANN TUCANA CLEAN
body: Model: Carol-Ann Tucana 3, Clean Channel
Orig. Cab: G12M, Classic Lead 80, Scholz
DynaCab: 1x12 Scholz
Controls: Input Level, Bass, Mid, Treble, Master, Presence
Preamp tubes: 12AX7A
Power tubes: KT88
Tonestack location: POST
Notes: “One of the best amps in the world IMO. I have Dumble, Trainwreck & various other boutique amps & Tucana is better than all of them” - Cliff
“Real world, has same amount of actual gain as Triptik. Way more than most people’s need for recording or live playing.”

Tonally, designed to cover many styles, suited to session players/players who need very wide palette of excellent tones. With 3 channels, can stray into higher gain levels, yet still retain full note definition & separation even with complex chord passages & ultra-quiet noise floor. 1st new feature is separate Input Level Controls for Clean & OD channels, gives greater flexibility than any previous models. Possible to set Clean Channel for little more crunch or maximum clean headroom.
Power amp section runs KT88s at moderate 75W, ensuring long tube life. Transformer set designed to retain ton of warmth, tightness in low end not previously seen in series.” - Carol-Ann

“Very difficult to get bad tone out of real amp, designed that way, range & limits on controls, even biasmon system make it difficult for even very inexperienced players to FU. Real amp has more British overtone, due to fact I’m British & designed it. Gain staging, OD voicing & output stage night & day to ANYTHING Mesa, any owner’d attest to that” - Alan Phillips

“You don’t need to crank real amp to get ton of OD. I designed it original for myself & play in a rock cover band. There’s nowhere I’d ever be able to get amp to point of pushing power tubes into OD. You gain stage a design based on use. For a design that needs a lot of OD at low volume, you do most of that/harmonic makeup in preamp, Tucana falls into this category. Master volumes in 500 seat club, rarely get above 10 o’clock on real amp, at that point, there’s still tons of clean headroom. Power amp adds to harmonic content, but very little OD. Tucana has high-pass filter in power amp feedback loop, for nice tight low end, as bass is subjected to more gain. For amp designed to push hard into power tube OD, this should be omitted, as all bets are off in feedback loop when you hit the rails. In other words, filter requires headroom to function properly, as does presence control of any amp where a component of feedback loop.” - Alan Phillips
Master Volume amp, distortion’s created by preamp tubes, not in power amp. MV works in power amp section still very important to tone & feel.
Tips: “Input gain 10:00, Gain 2:00 is 70s/80s lead tone, Input Level 2 o’clock for sustained lead tone/modern heavy rhythm tone” - Alan Phillips

“Preference of speaker is Celestion Creamback 65, I do gig with 2x12 Celestion T75’s. Pretty forgiving on speakers vs some models” & “Very tolerant of most cabs/speakers, unlike say OD2. Approach this model very simply. Don’t mess with extended parameters, don’t try & redesign it. Use reliable Celestion cab. I have a fantastic 4x12 with 2 old greenbacks & 2 old v30s, best cab I’ve played real amp through.” – Alan Phillips

amps.name: CAROL-ANN TUCANA LEAD
body: Model: Carol-Ann Tucana 3, Lead Channel

amps.name: CITRUS A30 CLEAN
body: Model: Orange AD30HTC, Clean channel
Orig. Cab: Orange 2x12 (V30)
DynaCab: 4x12 Citrus
Controls: Gain, Master, Bass, Mid, Treble
Preamp tubes: ECC83
Power tubes: EL84
Tonestack location: POST

Notes: “Essentially two vintage British-style amps in one, the valve rectified twin channel AD Series recaptures the warm, time-honored EL84 sound that defined an era. These amps have gained deserved reputation among Country pickers, Bluesmen, Pop, Funk & Indie artists for their classy, bell-like cleans, breaky crunch & controlled natural compression. This amp has proved to be so popular worldwide that its specification remains unchanged & is fast becoming the new standard in 30 watt Class A amplification.

With 2 completely separate signal paths, each with 2 stages of gain & master volume controls, both channels can be set clean or coaxed into varying degrees of crunch. Channel 1 has a slightly mellower character, with a thick, creamy, classic voice

However you set the controls, the AD30HTC remains wonderfully receptive to various guitars & playing styles, whilst the GZ34 valve rectifier imparts its own distinct vibe for players seeking a more vintage feel & responsiveness. Pair with our PPC212OB for glassy highs & spacious cleans or expose the AD30HTC’s heavier nature with our PPC212 or PPC412 for more low end projection.” – Orange
Lots of gain available but AD30 isn’t a high gain amp
Amp has MV, distortion created by preamp/power tubes

I use it for my ‘better than a Vox’ sounds. Has a real nasty bite to it, like chucking a bag of nails down some stairs, my ref tone is “What’s Going On“ by Taste (I know that’s an AC30 but it’s the same ‘bite’ to me). I discovered the amp after going through TAF Platinum bundle presets. There’s 1 in there called “Indie Yeah” that combines AD30 with Tayden True Brit IR. I’d never thought about using the amp before & I’d never heard of Tayden speakers before, but this combo just works for me. Maybe it’s a result of growing up in the UK with the sound of Oasis everywhere, I find the distorted tone really works for those Britpop arpeggio chords.” – simviz
Tips: Tone ctrls affect gain amount, turn up for more gain
“4x12 Citrus is 1 of my favorite 4x12/V30 stock cabs” - yek

amps.name: CITRUS A30 DIRTY
body: Model: Orange AD30HTC, Dirty channel (2)
Notes: Tighter bass response, faster attack, more gain & prominent upper mids
See 090 Citrus A30 for info

amps.name: CITRUS BASS 200
body: Model: Orange AD200B bass amp
Orig. Cab: Orange bass cab
DynaCab: 1x15 Heart Key
