amps.name: CITRUS RV50
body: Model: Orange Rockerverb 50 MKII, Dirty Channel
Orig. Cab: Orange 4x12 (V30)
DynaCab: 4x12 Citrus
Controls: Volume, Gain, Treble, Middle, Bass
Preamp tubes: ECC83
Power tubes: EL34
Tonestack location: POST
Notes: Slipknot’s Jim Root is a well-known Rockerverb user.
People often think that 50 watt is half as loud as 100 watt. But that’s not the case. The difference often is just a few decibels.
“All Valve, channel-switching amplifier with ultra-transparent effects loop & valve-driven reverb. This amplifier utilizes a 2 stage clean channel with a four stage dirty channel.

Clean Channel delivers a super rich clean tone with beautiful harmonic overtones. It is a non-master channel, much like vintage Orange amps & when cranked the power section breaks up faster, delivering the most classic British crunch imaginable.

The Dirty Channel is four stages of unadulterated filth, but it is NOT a one-trick pony. Move the Gain knob from 0 to 10 & as you do you’ll find a variety of Orange tones, from metal to fusion, rock to blues, there’s even some country in there.

All 50 watt Rockerverb Mark II amps offer switchable output valves. This means you can swap the factory fitted EL34 output valves to 6L6s, KT88s, or 6550s. Just make sure you take your amp to a tech for rebiasing!” – Orange

“Warmth, tonal quality & rich harmonics generated by a valve amp cannot be reproduced by ‘artificial’ means. Many guitarists have reached the same conclusion: neither the transistor nor microchip is a suitable alternative to valve technology.”
“The power amp in a Rockerverb has very little negative feedback so it will boost the highs more than most power amps.”
“Not a super high gain amp. Called a Rockerverb, not Metalverb. It’s got 4 gain stages & significant padding between stages”
(Gain) “It’s a ganged pot so it behaves strangely”
Tips: Master Volume means amp’s distortion is created by the

amps.name: CITRUS TERRIER
body: Model: Orange Tiny Terror
Orig. Cab: Orange 1x12/2x12 (G12H)
DynaCab: 2x12 Texas Star
Controls: Vol, Gain, Tone
Preamp tubes: ECC83
Power tubes: EL84
Tonestack location: PRE
Notes: “The actual amp has no tone stack & a single tone control. The tone control is actually a high cut control in the power amp & is therefore replicated by the Hi Cut parameter.”

The Tiny Terror is a simple, portable amp. Single channel, Class A, EL84 & switchable between 7 & 15 watt. At 15 watt it is still loud enough to keep up with a drummer & fill a room when combined with an appropriate cabinet. Reportedly based on Vox AC-15 (non-TB channel) with extra gain stage

“Often imitated, but never equaled, the iconic Tiny Terror quite literally turned the guitar world upside down upon its launch. Heralded by Guitarist Magazine as ‘the most important guitar product in the last 30 years‘, Tiny Terror ownership now stretches to well over 30.000 players worldwide. This is the original ‘lunchbox’ amp head & continues to set the standard by which all other low wattage valve amplifiers must be judged.

Combining ultra-portability with outstanding array of valve tones, amp head delivers exceptional range of British valve tones possible by a single channel with just 3 controls. Unique preamp section & gain structure for clean/bluesy crunch, Punk & Classic Rock, controllable by guitar’s volume knob.
Intuitive tone circuit acts as a roll off for the upper freqs while always retaining the character of the instrument, even when driving the amp to its absolute limits.

While philosophy behind Terror series began with musician on the move, their huge sonic range, supreme usability & switchable output options has made them go- to amps for studio engineers & producers” – Orange
The Tiny Terror combo includes a G12H speaker. But it works well with larger Orange cabs with V30 speakers as well. We have several Orange (Citrus) stock cabs.

Tips: The amp only does dirty tones, it’s inaudible when clean. The Tone control is actually a high-cut control in the power amp & is replicated by the Hi Cut parameter in the amp model. Hi Cut set fully CCW is equivalent to the amp’s tone knob fully CW. Keep T/M/B at noon for authentic sounds
Modeled in 15w mode. Model uses neutral tone stack. T/M/B to noon for authentic sounds/set to taste
Really an amp for dirty tones only, useless for clean tones
“Of Citrus amps, I like this best. Gain ~ 7, Hi-Cut 6 & control gain with guitar’s volume knob. That’s all” - yek
“4x12 Citrus is one of my fav 4x12/V30 stock cabs” - yek

Notes: The AD200B isn’t a “bells & whistles” kind of amp. It’s incredibly simple. Three tone knobs, gain & Master. You can choose between a passive or active input. It runs on 6550 tubes (valves, as they call them in the U.K.), four of them, rated 200w

“The AD200B MK 3 is quite simply one of the purest valve bass amps ever produced, designed on premise that the best tone often comes from the shortest signal path from guitar to speaker. Classic, uncomplicated design features inputs for both active & passive instruments, with a simple but effective EQ.

With 4 6550 output valves pushing 200w of power, AD200B has a creamy, dynamic low-end with a focused mid-range. Rounded gain structure reveals a wide spectrum of vintage & modern tones, from mellow hues perfect for flatwound strings, through to heavy percussive grind. Whatever the situation, there is no shortage of punch, which is just one reason why AD200B has appeared on some of the world’s biggest stages.” – Orange
Tips: Gain control enables you to dial in an overdriven bass tone g8x10 1200w cabinet is so big that yo’ mama could live in it g10” speakers are from Eminence, not found in FAS stock cabs

amps.name: CLASS-A 15W TB
body: Model: Vox AC15 Top Boost
Orig. Cab: Vox 1x12 (G12M,Alnico)
DynaCab: 1x12 AC20
Controls: Vol, Cut, Bass, Treble
Preamp tubes: 12AX7A (Syl)
Power tubes: EL84
Tonestack location: POST

Notes: From Beatles to nowadays rock stars, everybody plays VOX amps: Brian May, The Edge, Rory Gallagher, Radiohead, Kings of Leon, Ritchie Blackmore etc. Did you know that the famous James Bond theme was recorded on an AC15? & for some divine reason, VOX is the tone of choice in P&W
The AC15 was developed before the AC30. The AC15 simply was not loud enough so VOX made a louder version: the AC30

“The Vox AC30 garnered more attention for years, but in this age of reduced stage volumes & project-studio recording practices, the smaller AC15 has become even more desirable in some players’ estimation. Dick Denney’s creation benefited from several design elements that were specifically intended to flatter the voice of the electric guitar, rather than merely amplify it & these babies achieve that in spades. Plug into stout EF86 pentode preamp channel for creamy, milkshake-thick tones or ECC83 channel for more jangle & sparkle & you quickly hear what all the fuss is about.”
The AC15 & AC30 were rather dark amps, with a single tone control. The “Brian May” tone. The Top Boost circuit was added later, adding gain & Bass & Treble controls & the well-known VOX “chime”.
The VOX is often cited to be a Class A amp. I’ll quote Korg/VOX about the difference between Class A & Class A/B amps:
“In order to understand difference between these 2 types of amp designs (there are others), must 1st understand a little about tubes.

The most basic tube used as an amplifier consists of 3 elements: Cathode, Plate & Grid. The Cathode is heated (by the heater, another element in all tubes except in very old designs where the cathode is the heater) & forms a cloud of negatively charged electrons. The Plate has a positive charge that attracts the electrons. The Grid is the audio input to the tube & usually controls the flow of electrons.

Amplification happens when a signal is applied to the grid that allows for & controls how much current flows through to the plate. Because the signal voltage is relatively low & the plate voltage is relatively high (as supplied by the power supply), the small changes produced by the audio signal at the Grid appear much larger at the Plate, hence amplification.
In a Class A circuit, a positive voltage is applied to the Grid, which controls the flow of electrons. In this circuit design current is flowing at all times through the tube.

In a Class “AB” design a negative “bias” voltage is applied to the grid, which will cause the tube to “shut off” when the audio waveform is below a certain point. Meanwhile there is another tube & associated circuit that is turning on before the first one turns off & is reproducing the rest of the waveform. In short these two tubes share the job of reproducing the full audio waveform.
Each type of design has its advantages & disadvantages.” Class A advantages are:
The tube is ready to amplify the signal at all times

Signal is instantaneously amplified because tube doesn’t have to “wake up” from a less than full operational state g30w “Class A” amp will sound louder than a 30w Class AB amp preamp & power tubes. You have to decide for yourself what setting in the amp model works for you best.
Orange’s 2x12 & 4x12 cabs are equipped with V30 speakers. There’s several Orange (Citrus) stock cabs

amps.name: CLASS-A 30W
body: Model: Vox AC-30, AC30HW head, Normal channel
Orig. Cab: Vox 2x12 (Alnico, G12M)
DynaCab: 2x12 Class-A 30W
Controls: Vol (drive), Bright switch, Treble, Bass, Tone Cut (hi-cut)
Preamp tubes: 12AX7A (Syl)
Power tubes: EL84
Tonestack location: POST

Notes: From Beatles to nowadays rock stars, everybody plays VOX amps: Brian May, The Edge, Rory Gallagher, Radiohead, Kings of Leon, Ritchie Blackmore etc & for some divine reason, tone of choice in P&W. The Edge’s famous amp is ’64 Top Boost AC30/6 model

AC30 originally was a dark 2-channel amp with 1 tone control. The “Brian May” tone. People use treble boosters to, duh, boost treble, which also added gain. Top Boost circuit, aka “Brilliance”, was added later, adds Bass/Treble controls & creating well-known VOX chime
AC30 has High & Low inputs per channel. If original amp has both inputs, the amp model is always based on the High input. To get the equivalent of the Low input, decrease Input Trim in the amp model.
“While Class-A models (VOX) are outstanding, there’s boutique amp models supposedly “better than VOX” (Morgan, Ruby, Trainwreck)”
“AC30’s have no negative feedback on power amp, so output voltage follows speaker impedance. Therefore you get a boost at low freq resonance of speaker, causes low end to get loose when cranked”
Tips: To increase gain, turn up Input Drive. What also works well with VOX amps is boost signal at input stage, ex: by increasing Input Trim, or by adding a Drive, like FET Boost or SDD

“Note that particular AC30 used for model has scratchy high end, reflected in the high Dyn Pres value. It’s a hand-wired version & that can lead to this type of sound as parasitics cause boost of very high freqs when amp driven hard. Obvious solution, turn Dyn Pres down”

“I intentionally limited midrange notch as it’s, IMO, a design flaw in that tone stack. Can recover behavior: set Mid 0. Modern ones don’t have this, they use Fender-style tonestack with fixed mid resistor”
A VOX AC30 typically feeds 2 Alnico Blues or 2 greenbacks. Alnico speakers are often used when bright tones are needed

Notes: See 096 Class-A 30w for info

amps.name: COMET 60
body: Model: Komet 60
Orig. Cab: Komet’s 2x12 (Classic Lead 80)
DynaCab: 2x12 Lead 80
Controls: Volume, Treble, Middle, Bass, Presence, Hi Cut
Preamp tubes: 12AX7A
Power tubes: EL34
Tonestack location: PRE

Notes: After Dumble, most expensive guitar amps are Trainwrecks. Those boutique amps were created by (the late) Ken Fischer. He designed amps for Komet too, which are also held in high esteem & similar to Trainwreck amps

“Komet is a 100% new design, but built along traditional Trainwreck methodologies. Like all “Wrecks”, Komet is a single channel amp. NO reverb, master volume, pull pots, transistors or chips ANYWHERE.”

“Amp produces about 50 to 60 Trainwreck watt of power. Amp has plenty of headroom, uses 5AR4/GZ34 tube rectifier for feel & sound this tube creates, without mushy sag often associated with tube rectification. Bass response is big, bold, clear & tight, touch response adjustable from gradual (like 50s/60s amp), to fast, like a Trainwreck. Amp’s workmanship is second to none” - Ken Fischer
Komet 60 is Komet’s flagship model. EL34 power tubes but can swap these for other tubes. Rated at 50-60w.
“Komet is definitely a unique amp. Sorta like a Fender preamp into a Marshall power amp. Unique tone stack.”

Volume followed by standard 3-band EQ, presence & high cut control. High cut operates within power section & allows user to darken & shape overall tone of amp without affecting EQ of the preamp, solving the common problem of loss of gain/clarity turning treble down in the preamp

Touch Response switch circuit enables altering amp’s touch response character to suit playing style. Choose from fast or gradual response to picking dynamics & guitar volume control settings. Circuit isn’t an extra gain stage or gain boost circuit, works by magnifying subtle changes in pick/finger attack with subtle changes in guitar volume. Allows clean tones to run full range of dynamics without breaking into unwanted distortion. Blues players can play on edge of distortion, going from clean to blues OD using pick attack as the controlling factor. Over the top players can go from clean to scorch using fast setting. Slightest change in pick attack/guitar volume translates to big changes in dynamics & power. Pick response/feel wanted, make amp capable of playing any music style” – Komet
Tips: Touch Response switch lets you choose from Fast & Gradual response. Model based Fast position. Replicate Gradual position: set Input Trim to .25 & turn down Bass
Lot of bass, so turn it down, or use Cut switch

EL34 stock factory setup for benchmark English era rock & roll. 6L6s for 60’s American rock. EL34s, KT77s, 6L6s, 5881s, KT66s, KT88s, 6550s, tube choice decides where sound takes you. Expect complex harmonics, bold/clean focused clarity & aggressive when pushed. Balanced full tonal range, no matter the power/preamp tube selected
Komet 4x12 cabs with choice of following speakers: G12H, G12M (greenbacks), Alnico Blue or Gold & G12-65

amps.name: COMET CONCOURSE
body: Model: Komet Concorde
Orig. Cab: Komet’s 2x12 has Classic Lead 80
DynaCab: 2x12 Lead 80
Controls: Volume, Treble, Middle, Bass, Presence, Hi Cut
Preamp tubes: 12AX7A
Power tubes: EL34
Tonestack location: PRE
Notes: “Komet is definitely a unique amp. Sorta like Fender preamp into Marshall power amp. Unique tone stack.”

“The Komet is a 100% new design, but is built along traditional Trainwreck methodologies. Like all “Wrecks”, the Komet is a single channel amp. It has NO reverb, master volume, effects loop, foot switches, pull pots, transistors or chips ANYWHERE.”
We already discussed the Komet 60. The Concorde’s specs are quite similar: EL34s (but not swappable), 50/60 watt, etc. More than the 60, the Concorde is positioned as a rock amp.

“Traditional Trainwreck principles are followed in the design & construction. The Concorde is a single channel amp & does not have reverb, a master volume, an effects loop, foot switches, pull pots, transistors, chips or anything else that would degrade tone, harmonic complexity or touch sensitivity.

This Trainwreck circuit, which includes Ken Fischer’s specially designed power & output transformers, offers an exceptional sound. The Concorde is designed to operate exclusively on EL 34 power tubes. Ken has achieved the very best from this tube compliment without any compromises. The Concorde has more gain than Ken Fischer’s other designs for Komet & incorporates a solid state rectifier. A new circuit in conjunction with new transformers & the careful selection of only the finest components allow the Concorde to offer benchmark EL 34 tone with rich complex harmonics, creamy rhythms & singing leads - all reinforced with a strong, bold, tight, low-end response. The Concorde follows the Komet / Trainwreck tradition of creating an amplifier with rich, balanced sound & unmatched musicality including superb touch sensitivity, note articulation, string to string separation & dynamic response.” – Komet

The amp has a Touch Response switch which lets you choose between Fast & Gradual response. The model is based on the switch in the “Fast” position. You can replicate the “Gradual” position by decreasing Input Trim to .250 in the amp model.
While Komet writes that the Concorde has more gain than the 60, it seems to be the other way around with the amp models: the “60” model has (slightly) more gain than the Concourse.

“The amp should produce about 50 to 60 Trainwreck watt of power. The amp must have plenty of headroom. The amp must use a 5AR4/GZ34 tube rectifier for the feel & sound this tube creates without the mushy sag usually associated with tube rectification. The amp’s bass response must be big, bold, clear & tight. The amp’s touch response must be adjustable from gradual, like a fifties or sixties amp, to fast, like a Trainwreck amp. Amp’s workmanship is second to none” – Ken Fischer
Tips: Touch Response switch lets you pick from Fast & Gradual response. Model based on switch in Fast position. To replicate Gradual position: decrease Input Trim to .250
Lot of bass, so turn it down or use Cut switch
“Like Comet 60, model doesn’t need a lot of EQ. I have everything default, except Input Trim (see above) & Input Drive around 4.” – yek
“Amp doesn’t need lot of EQ IMO. I have everything at default, except Input Trim (see above), low Bass/Input Drive around 4” – yek
Komet builds 4x12 cabs, try these speakers: G12H, G12M, Alnico Blue or Gold & G12-65

amps.name: CORNFED M50
body: Model: Cornford MK50 II, OD channel
Orig. Cab: Cornford 4x12 (V30)
DynaCab: 4x12 Friedman V30
Controls: Gain (Input Drive), Overdrive, Bass, Middle, Treble, Volume, 2 MV, Presence & Resonance (Depth)
Preamp tubes: ECC83
Power tubes: EL34/6L6
Tonestack location: POST
Notes: “Plexi-meets-Modern tone with big cojones”

“I first heard & saw a Cornford amp when Dweezil Zappa played one at a Zappa Plays Zappa gig in The Netherlands. That was before he moved to Fractal Audio gear. His sound blew me away. As a matter of fact it blew Steve Vai’s tone away too, who was present too, playing his usual gear.” – yek
Cornford players: Dweezil Zappa, Richie Kotzen & Guthrie Govan
Corncob RK100 amp model was a popular in 1st gen Axe-Fx, based on Richie Kotzen’s signature amp. Model now based on another Cornford MK50 II, Cornford’s flagship at the time
MK50 II is a hand-built 3-channel amp, with EL34 (modeled) or 6L6 power tubes & a solid-state rectifier. Rated output is 50w

“Model I enjoyed playing most. Quite different vs other high-gain amps. It isn’t the amount of gain, but the “Bam! In your face!” tone. If I was playing in a hard rock band, this would be a top candidate
Has characteristics of other brands, like Marshall, MESA & Soldano, but retains its own vibe: big, brutal & bold, combines the best of all

OD channel has 2 gain controls (Input Drive & Overdrive). On amps like this, like Dumble, Fryette, Carol-Ann & MESA, 1st control sets sensitivity, 2nd sets amount of distortion. Models with Input Drive & Overdrive controls always feel elastic & spongy
Amp has Master, so amp’s distortion is created by preamp tubes, not power amp. MV works in power amp & important to tone & feel
Tips: Add Drive block (T808: Drive 0, Level 7) to make it less loose
Works great with model: put Drive in front, like T808, Drive 0 & Level 7. Tames bass & makes the tone even better” – yek
Try Input Drive 5, OD 8, Bass 0, Mid 5, Treble 5, Presence 3
Lot of bass in amp model. Turn it down or use Cut switch
Cornford cabs use V30 speakers. Alt, try 2 4x12 FAS stock cabs

amps.name: DAS METALL
body: Model: Diezel VH4 (schematics, ear-tuned, not matched)
Orig. Cab: Diezel 4x12 (V30, G12-100)
DynaCab: 4x12 Recto Straight
Controls: Gain, Vol, Bass, Mid, Treble, Pres(4k), Deep(80Hz)
Preamp tubes: ECC83
Power tubes: 6L6

amps.name: DELUXE 6G3
body: Model: Fender Deluxe,
Brownface 6G3, Bright channel
Orig. Cab:
DynaCab: 1x12 Deluxe Verb
Controls: Volume, Tone
Preamp tubes: 12AX7
Power tubes: 6V6
Tonestack location:

Notes: Fat switch controls value of a capacitor in tone stack. 6G3 doesn’t have a tone stack, so switch isn’t applicable. Bright switch & Bright cap control value of a capacitor across Drive pot. 6G3 doesn’t have a conventional drive circuit, so these aren’t applicable

For guitarists hip to what came in between, brown-panel 6G3 of ‘61-’63 is one of tastiest tone machines of bunch, packing best-of-both-worlds sounds that can twang, jangle, sparkle & roar, depending on how you attack it.

With 2 full gain stages before the phase inverter & those derived from a 7025 (aka 12AX7) preamp tube vs lower- gain 12AY7 of tweeds & very little signal loss from passive tone control, circuit can push hot signal into output stage

Amp’s back end was further altered from tweed-Deluxe template. More complex long-tailed-pair phase inverter for hifi response, more efficient fixed-bias setup for 2 6V6GTs/ GZ34s firmer rectification tube (vs 5E3’s 5Y3 valve).
Put it all together & you get meaty vintage-style OD from preamp cranked to throaty roar by output stage, for lead tones many compare to mini Marshall with American twist

Has tremolo effect predecessor never had. Powered by ½ of 2nd of 3 12AX7 in preamp, bias-modulating circuit applies its modulation to output tubes’ bias network. One of simpler tube-trem circuits ever, but a rich/warm sound

Many earlier examples of 6G3 came with highly regarded 12” Jensen P12Q speakers with Alnico magnets, but by the time of this Deluxe’s ‘62 release, Oxford 12K5 became standard issue. Still a good-sounding speaker & makes crisp delivery of what 6G3 has to offer when amp is kept at low-to-medium volumes

amps.name: DELUXE TWEED
body: Model: Fender Tweed Deluxe, 1957 narrow-panel 5E3, bright input
Orig. Cab: Fender 1x12 (Jensen P12R, C12N or Celestion Alnico Blue)
DynaCab: 1x12 Deluxe Tweed
Controls: Volume (Drive), Tone (Treble)
Preamp tubes: 12AX7A (Syl)
Power tubes: 6V6
Tonestack location: PRE
Notes: Tweed amplifiers typically break up earlier than later models & are known for their warm sounding distortion
Marketed as a studio amp because of its low wattage

“Rich & sweet clean tones at low volume, toothsome overdrive at decibels that won’t make the soundman apoplectic & compact dimensions that’ll let it ride in the front seat of your Fiat 500. Tweed Deluxe is the original ideal studio & club amp”
It’s a low output amp. 6V6 tubes generate 12 to 15w. It starts to distort early, attributed to its popularity & the reason why players such as Neil Young (who owns hundreds) & Billy Gibbons use it.

“Also note this amp suffers from extreme blocking distortion at or near max gain. This is common in very old designs. As it’s virtually unplayable like this, model uses a somewhat reduced level of grid conduction to lower the amount of blocking distortion & make the amp more playable at high Drive settings.” – firmware release notes

“5E3 Deluxe is desirable among players within many music styles. Tone is fat/rich with strong mids & decent bass response given relatively small cab size, approximately same size as Princeton Reverb. Construction of pine baffle & cab sounds tighter & more closed vs blackface open cabs, with possibility of more low end response given the right speaker. 12” speaker contributes to a full, warm tone & pure/simple tone stack of 5E3 Circuit delivers natural & uncolored tone of guitar

Known for its distorted & cranked tones, comes easily when volume/tone is above 12 o’clock. Early distortion partly by lack of negative feedback loop & 6V6 cathode bias design, which offers more sag & compression, less clean headroom than fixed bias (typically AB763 blackface amps & a few tweed amps). Besides fact that it distorts easily, what fascinates us most is golden tone secrets that exist within this amp. Experiment with tone knob & other channel’s volume knob (channel not plugged into), to find one of the most beautiful clean tones of all times & confirms Leo is a God of tone. Clean sound is not as loud, cold & terrifying as silverface/blackface era amps. They’ll require much more experience/effort to dial in sweet tones, but once you know how, you’re rewarded greatly. With 5E3 Deluxe, it’s easier to quickly dial in a nice clean tone, especially at low volume levels. Given early distortion & mellow clean sound, 5E3 Deluxe is great for single coil guitars, especially bright bridge pickup of Strat & Tele

Looking at the control panel, 5E3 Deluxe appears to be very simple. Few controls provide quick & small selection of tones. It’s practically impossible to not find a good tone with this amp. Just need to turn up volume & set tone. We believe the world would be a better place if the 5E3 Deluxe was the only amp around. All guitar players deserve to play this amp & learn how to create music with natural & transparent guitar tone. But amp isn’t really as simple as one might think. 2 interactive volume channels & mysterious, non-linear tone control provides a big spectrum of tones, everything from Fender clean to Marshall grit. Many players find the 2 volume controls confusing. We agree. What the heck was Leo & his engineers really thinking when they designed the 2 volume controls? When you plug into the normal channel any normal person, even a drummer, would expect the other channel’s setting isn’t affecting tone. But no. As you gradually turn the other volume knob, both tone & volume changes, drastically at very end between 10 & 12 where you’d expect nuthin more to come. It works likewise the other way around if you play the bright channel. Even more confusing is the tone control. You might expect a treble or mid control. Nope, tone control affects volume, gain, EQ/tone, everything & worse, it doesn’t behave consequently at different volume levels of 2 channels. In total you have 4 inputs to choose from, 2 volume knobs & a tone knob that you can spend years trying to figure out”
Tips: Just single Tone control, mapped to Treble in block, turn it up! Keep Bass, Mid & Master default for authenticity & play a telecaster
Each of the two channels (Normal & Bright) has two inputs. The model is based on the Bright channel & High input. To get the equivalent of the low input, set Input Trim to 0.500 in the amp model.
The amp only has a Volume control for each channel & a shared Tone control. In the amp model Tone is mapped to Treble. You will want to turn up Treble a lot (8 or higher)
“Note this amp only has a single tone control. This is modeled by Treble control in the Axe-Fx. Bass/Mid controls are functional & recreate the amp when set to noon”
Cranking volume, crazy distortion, uncontrollable, sounds like a fuzz.
“Yup, that’s the key. Crank the treble almost all the way up.”
Many people insist that a Deluxe must be used with a telecaster. A cranked Deluxe takes the harsh end off the bridge pickup & the low end of the neck pickup
The Deluxe combo included a single Jensen P12 Alnico speaker. Try the 1x12 Tweed stock cabs or other try Alnico stocks cabs.

amps.name: DELUXE TWEED JUMPED
body: Model: ‘57 narrow-panel Fender Tweed Deluxe 5E3 jumpered inputs
Notes: See 106 Deluxe Tweed for info

amps.name: DELUXE VERB NORMAL
body: Model: Fender DLX Rvrb g‘65 blackface AB763, Normal channel
Orig. Cab: Fender 1x12 with Jensen C12(K), EVM 12L, JBL D120 Or Fender 2x10 w/ Jensen C10N, C10Q or Jensen 25W Alnico P10R
DynaCab: 1x12 Deluxe Verb
Controls: Volume, Bass, Treble, Reverb effect
Preamp tubes: 7025 (12AX7A Syl)
Power tubes: 6V6
Tonestack location: PRE
Notes: Fender Deluxe Reverb, aka “DR”, isn’t same as Fender Deluxe with added reverb. It is, kinda, but amp sounds different, more scooped

“The Deluxe Reverb 1x12” has for decades been one of the most popular amps among all Fender amps & its popularity is still growing as the demand for low wattage tube amps increases. The combination of size, weight & performance makes the Deluxe Reverb a true road warrior on gigs & practice. Both the Deluxe Reverb (DR) & Princeton Reverb (PR) “survived” the CBS silverface periods with minor changes. Many people consider the silverface amps just as sonically good as the blackface models. Being almost a blackface amp with a new faceplate (wrong for collectors), the silverface models became popular value- for-the-money amps. Collectors hunt the blackface models & players hunt the silverface for its quality & price. DR is a small/medium-sized amp with an ability to deliver both sparkling clean tones & low wattage breakup at 22W with 6V6 tubes & relatively small transformers (power & output). It has found its way in to more recording studios, clubs & bars than any other Fender amp. It has met many players’ expectations. It is just powerful enough to cut through in practice in a band with a drummer and, unlike some bigger amps, it will reach its sweet spot at a reasonable volume. It has the typical blackface 60s sparkle with a scooped clean sound just like the bigger Fender brothers, just with more smooth breakup, sag & compression”

“An equally fine choice for a versatile small club amp is the Vibrolux Reverb’s little brother, the Blackface Deluxe Reverb (1964 through 1967). It is rated at just 24 watt or so (with two 6V6 power tubes), but the overall circuitry in the amp is virtually the same as the Vibrolux Reverb. It has two channels, but I only use the second channel, again, due to the extra stage of gain. It drives a single 12” speaker. Most of the Deluxe Reverbs that Fender built have a very inefficient Oxford 12” in them. The first thing you should do to make this amp really worthy is to install a much larger ceramic magnet speaker. A better 12” is essential to having a Deluxe sound as good as it possibly can (and that is very good indeed.) The Deluxe Reverb will not have as much bottom as a Vibrolux Reverb, due to its smaller power tubes, but anyone who’s heard a Deluxe Reverb set at about “6” for leads knows how sweet the 6V6 power tubes sound. Again, you need to run this amp at “6” or above in terms of volume to really bring out the classic overdriven Fender tone. I find that setting my guitar’s volume knob on “10” for leads works wonderfully, then when I back it off to say “7” or so for rhythm, the amp naturally cleans up. That’s a neat trick to use. If you are hesitant to change your original speaker, consider acquiring a second speaker cabinet for your new & improved speaker. But remember, amps are tone machines, not museum pieces, so if you are torn between swapping out the speaker, keep in mind that you can always store it away & reinstall it if you decide to sell the amp later. I have an extension cabinet with a 15” Electrovoice SRO speaker in it & I sometimes run my own Deluxe Reverb through that setup. It dramatically improves the tone—huge fat bottom, low mids & much more overall volume.” – ToneQuest

“22-watt ’65 Deluxe Reverb amp is for rock, country or blues players who want a moderately powered amp they can crank up at the gig or in the studio. The full, snappy guitar tones heard on many famous recordings from Memphis to Abbey Road were made using the Deluxe amp. Features include dual 6V6 Groove Tubes output tubes, one 5AR4 rectifier tube, four 12AX7 preamp tubes, two 12AT7 tubes, one 12” 8-ohm Jensen C-12K speaker, dual channels (normal & vibrato), tube-driven Fender reverb, tube vibrato, two button footswitch for reverb & vibrato on-off, black textured vinyl covering & silver grille cloth.”

Deluxe Reverb has Normal & Vibrato channels, both modeled because of different characteristics. Most people use Vibrato channel but Normal channel is pretty cool too, especially for single note runs & jazzy tunes.
“Values for Deluxe Reverb are based on measurements of actual 65 Deluxe Reverb, not some hypothetical values on some spec sheet.”

amps.name: DELUXE VERB VIBRATO
body: Model: 1965 blackface Fender DLX Reverb, AB763, Vibrato channel
See 108 Deluxe Verb for more info

amps.name: DIAMANTE FIRE
body: Model: 22W Diamond Del Fuego
Orig. Cab: single GH12H, V30
DynaCab: 1x12 Hot Kitty
Controls: Volume, Tone, Tremolo effect
Preamp tubes: 12AX7
Power tubes: EL84
Tonestack location: POST
Notes: based on a ‘60s Class-A 18W Marshall
“Nasty little thing.”
“Yeah, it’s a cool little amp. It’s got some different component values than the Leeds which gives a different vibe.”

Del Fuego is hand-built in time-honored tradition of famous/sought-after early ‘60s British model. Hand wired on hand-built fiberglass eyelet board using components selected for reliability/superior sonic character. 3 12AX7 & 2 EL84 power tubes

Amp is Class A at its finest, so hand made that Diamond literally fabricates the eyelet boards it’s built on. So is characteristic of Diamond, only the finest of components used. True tube, true Class A, true clean/blues/vintage breakup, true Texas

“They are very dark amp when used with the matching 16-ohm cab. The feedback is off the speaker jack (don’t ask me why, doesn’t make sense). This results in a lot of negative feedback at high frequencies which makes the sound dark. The amp is modeled assuming a 16-ohm load. If you use them into an 8-ohm cabinet they’re brighter. To simulate this lower the negative feedback by 1/sqrt(2). This results in a negative feedback value of 3”

“A favorite of mine. I use it for everything, from clean to higher gain. Funky & rhythm stuff at low drive settings, light crunch with medium drive, higher drive settings to get it into rock territory & searing leads
Model works with lot of speakers, like G12M, G12-65, G12H & V30. For Mark Day’s cab sound used with his Friedman BE, use stock cabs 4x12 Fractal (V30s & greenbacks)

amps.name: DIRTY SHIRLEY 1
body: Model: 40W Friedman Dirty Shirley, current version
Orig. Cab: Friedman 4x12 (V30, G12M, G12-65, G12H)
DynaCab: 4x12 Friedman GB
Controls: Gain (Drive), Master, Bass, Middle, Treble, Presence
Preamp tubes: 12AX7A (JJ)
Power tubes: 6L6/5881
Tonestack location: POST
Notes: “Dave Friedman’s Dirty Shirley 40w Head was designed for guitarists wanting Vintage Classic Rock tone inspired by British tube amps of ‘60s/70s

It is based on a modified JTM 45 but still delivers the signature high gain tone for which Friedman is best known. The Dirty Shirley is a 40-watt, 5881 powered, single channel amp that will give you that vintage crunch & also cleans up nicely by merely backing off the guitar’s volume knob. Extremely versatile, it is capable of producing many styles of music from blues to rock & country by just adjusting the gain & master controls.” – Friedman

The Dirty Shirley is based on the Marshall JTM 45, puts out 40 watt, has a single channel, high & low sensitivity inputs & 5881 (6L6) power tubes. It sounds more classic & vintage than Friedman’s flagship, the BE
Compared to JTM 45, Friedman is less bright, much fatter, more gain & Master

“The design is weird as the negative feedback is taken off the speaker jack. So if you use a 16-ohm speaker you get more feedback (and much more bass) than if you use an 8-ohm speaker. It’s too much bass for me so I turn down the Depth & reduce Negative Feedback a bit.”

“At 1st I didn’t like it but for some genres it works. The old version (new model) has 2.2 nF cap on the cathode follower while the newer version has 4.7 nF. The larger cap smooths out the distortion. Cathode bypass cap values are different as well which changes the midrange response.”
“Some amps have a fixed depth circuit, like 5153, Freidman BE/HBE, Dirty Shirley, TripTik, Tucana & default value of Depth corresponds to fixed circuit”
“Dirty Shirley 1 is actually the latest version of the amp”
“They are dark amps”

Maybe this amp’s heritage is why I like the model so much. It’s based on the JTM 45 (another excellent amp / model), which in turn is based on the Fender Bassman. Dirty Shirley has a vibe of its own, it’s vintage, not as smooth & polished & modern as others. It also cleans up well & cuts well through my guitar cab as well as FR monitors.

“More attracted to guitar tones that are a little filthy & raw. Too many clips demonstrate perfectly EQ’d tones, TBH they don’t stick, I find them boring & don’t remember them, there’s no personality. Dirty Shirley does!” - yek
Tips: “We recommend starting amp with Bass 6, mids 7-8 & treble/presence 5, gain ~6 & bring master to taste” – Manual

“For more clarity in the low mids, reduce Negative Feedback. Friedman’s designs are a bit odd & takes the feedback off the speaker jack rather than off a transformer tap. The amp will sound tighter & clearer in the low mids”
Amp has MV, amp’s distortion created by preamp tubes. Master works in power amp, still important for tone & feel
Friedman amps sound great at low Master settings
If too much bass, decrease Depth or Negative Feedback

“I always lower negative feedback. Friedman amps are a bit strange, as negative fdbk is taken off the speaker jack instead of one of the transformer taps. Matching cabs are 16ohms, but 8ohm cab has 30% less feedback”
Love that it’s basic & simple. I don’t turn a lot of knobs. I dial back Bass/Depth to decrease bass freqs & turn up Presence in clean parts. Eat that, MESA

“We got our reference Deluxe Reverb out of storage today, hooked it up & measured & compared. Gain measures spot-on & A/B testing sounds identical. With a Suhr Modern guitar with stock pickups both the amp & the model were starting to break up around 3 (which equals 2.2 on the Axe-Fx since the knobs start at zero not one). A Deluxe Reverb breaks up easily since it has 6V6 power tubes. The preamp is virtually identical to a Twin Reverb but 6V6s break up earlier than 6L6s. For a given voltage into the power amp a 6V6 has about 70% of the headroom compared to a 6L6.”
Tips: Chimey tone, breaks up nicely when pushed
A specific way to dial in this amp is to turn Bass all the way & set Drive at its maximum value
Each channel has 2 inputs. Fractal models always based on input with highest input level. To get equivalent of using the lower input, set Input Trim to 0.500 in the amp model
Each channel has Volume, Bass & Treble controls (plus Reverb & Vibrato controls on Vibrato channel). No Bright switch & no Master so keep these at default in amp block
Deluxe Reverb often equipped with Jensen 1x12” speaker, like C12, try 1x12 Deluxe Verb or 1x12 Black
Alternatives: 2x10” speakers, EV-12L or JBL 120

amps.name: DIRTY SHIRLEY 2
body: Model: 40W Friedman Dirty Shirley, earlier version, different component values, lil more aggro vs 111 model
Notes: “Brighter model(2.2nF cathode cap instead of 4.7)”

“At first I didn’t like it but for some genres it works. Old version (new model) 2.2 nF cap on cathode follower while newer version has 4.7 nF. Larger cap smooths out distortion. Cathode bypass cap values are different, changes midrange response”
See 111 Dirty Shirley for info

amps.name: DIV/13 CJ
body: Model: Divided By 13 CJ 11
Orig Cab: Div by 13 G12M
DynaCab: 1x12 Div13 CJ11
Controls: Vol (Drive), Mid Gain Boost, Treble, Bass, MV
Preamp tubes: 12AX7A (Syl)
Power tubes: 6V6
Tonestack location: PRE
Notes: similar to Tweed Dlx, with more clean headroom

“When asked, by Corky James, amongst others, “Can you take my favorite ‘59 tweed amp from Fullerton & instead of Volume/Tone controls, make it Volume/Treble/Bass, with more clean headroom, wider range of usable OD (throw in a EL34), a little louder, with fuller, tighter low end & put a master volume in it so I can play at all levels & dial in & hit the sweet spot of whatever speaker i am using at the time, mostly a G12M Celestion.” – Divided by 13
The CJ 11 is a Class A amp with 6V6 power tubes & a Master. It’s only 11 watt. It’s a single channel amp but its boost (pull Volume) makes it versatile. It has two inputs
Tips: Bassy amp, works best with single coils. Turn up MV
“You increase the LOW CUT FREQ to reduce the fuzziness on bass notes but then it won’t sound like the real thing.”
“While the amp does offer an Master Volume, it sounds best to me to crank the Master & leave it there.” – yek
Combo has a single G12M. IR stocks cab: 1x12 Division 13

amps.name: DIV/13 CJ BOOST
body: Model: Divided By 13 CJ11, mid gain boost on
Notes: See 113 DIV/13 CJ for more info

amps.name: DIV/13 FT37 HIGH
body: Model: Divided By 13 FTR 37w
Channel 1, Mid Gain Boost on
Orig. Cab: Divided by 13 (G12H/Alnico)
DynaCab: 2x12 Chiefman
Controls: Volume (Drive) Pull: Mid Gain Boost, Treble, Bass
Preamp tubes: 12AX7A (Syl)
Power tubes: 6V6
Tonestack location: PRE
Notes: 1st channel is voiced like big clear Fender

“Capturing & exceeding some of the best tones offered by our favorite amp from Fullerton & favorite amp from England, FTR 37 from 1 to 10 can produce most pure & bodied clean tones, to varying degrees of medium & over-the-top types of complex & full-bodied OD. Coupled with exterior switching/boost device, Switchazel, flexibility available in both channels, either separate or blended, offer infinite array of guitar tones rarely thought possible for music maker of today.” – Divided by 13
The amp’s controls are simple: Volume, Treble, Bass on the first channel. Pull out Volume for a mid/gain boost. On the 2nd channel: .
The “LO” amp model represents channel 1 with the Volume knob pushed (gain boost off). The “HI” amp model represents channel 1 with the Volume knob pulled (mid/gain boost).
Tips: Crank Input Drive for a great tone
The 2x12 cabinet has Alnico & a G12H speakers.
“Sounds great w/ Input Drive 5-9, esp High w/ high drive setting”-yek

amps.name: DIV/13 FT37 LOW
body: Model: Divided By 13 FTR 37w, Low: channel 1, Mid Gain Boost: off
Controls: Volume & Tone knob (referred as “Click” as it clicks between settings). No Master Volume, keep at default in amp block
Notes: See 115 DIV/13 FT37 for more info
Tips: “Sounds great with Input Drive 5-9” - yek

amps.name: DIZZY V4 BLUE 2
body: Model: Diezel VH4, Diezel VH4 Blue, Crunch
Orig. Cab: Diezel 4x12 (V30, G12-100)
DynaCab: 4x12 Recto Straight
Controls: Gain, Volume, Bass, Mid, Treble, Presence (4k), Deep (80Hz)
Preamp tubes: ECC83
Power tubes: 6L6/KT77
Tonestack location: POST
Notes: “Reason Diezels compress so much is due to cathode follower design. To reduce: lower Compression in Cathode Follower section”
“Bright switch on Silver version chnl 2 isn’t conventional bright switch. Switches in treble peaker later in circuit. Model has bright switch on”

“Humans perceive high freq thermal noise as hiss. More gain=more hiss. Diezel has lotta filtering, large grid stoppers, plate caps, etc reduces bandwidth & lowers hiss. Likewise, Camerons are pretty wide open, small grid stoppers, bypass caps on grid stoppers, no plate caps, etc”
Users: Metallica, Mark Tremonti, Muse, Adam Jones (Tool)
Diezel Hifi clean, gain pot can reproduce powerful/warmer Hiwatt sound

amps.name: DIZZY V4 BLUE 3
body: Model: Diezel VH4 Blue, Distortion/Mega

Notes: Channel 3 Distortion: its concept’s heavy rhythm guitar. Distortion is noticeably tighter vs chnl 2. Side effect of added compression is signal gets somewhat limited in dynamic range, limitation hits mostly lower freqs, loss is corrected by negative feedback loop to power amps, adds fat low end to tone without muddling up tone. Control for this low end is in master section & labeled “Deep”. Gain structure designed to deliver great distorted sound even with relatively low output guitars, like many vintage guitars. Side effect is that guitars with very high output (active EMG, etc) might OD channel too much & become too compressed. Guitar loses its punch in band sound & becomes very undefined, in this case, radical gain reduction is cure. Guitar sound cleans up some & overtones/harmonics will again sparkle. Best to start with 12:00 o’clock Gain & start to feel way to point of perfection & little compression. Works best while band’s playing with you. Remember: less is often more
Notes: See 117 Dizzy V4 for info

amps.name: DIZZY V4 BLUE 4
body: Model: Diezel VH4 Blue, Solo/Lead

Notes: Channel 4 Solo: Little more mid heavy vs ch 3, punches through band quite well. See ch 3 description for gain control setting. Often gain near 1:00 is sufficient for absolute high gain distortion. Use volume control & other channels to match individual channel volumes”

amps.name: DIZZY V4 SILVER 2
body: Model: Diezel VH4 Silver, Crunch

Notes: Crunch channel (with bright switch) starts where clean ends: warm, powerful clean sounds. Variation of controls (volume, gain) will open amp to sonic dimensions of semi-crunch & full crunch sounds
Tips: “I go for Silver (which is why I never became an Olympic athlete).” – yek

amps.name: DIZZY V4 SILVER 3
body: Model: Diezel VH4 Silver, Distortion/Mega
Notes: Diezel trademark sound. Nuff said. Try it, love it.

amps.name: DIZZY V4 SILVER 4
body: Model: Diezel VH4 Silver, Solo/Lead
Notes: Starts where mega ends: more comp/smoothness, singing/screaming lead at its best

amps.name: DOUBLE VERB NORMAL
body: Model: ‘66 blackface Fender Twin Reverb
Tuned by Andy Fuchs, AB763 circuit, Normal channel
Orig. Cab: Fender 2x12 with original speakers
DynaCab: 2x12 Double Verb
Controls: Volume, Bass, Middle, Treble, Bright switch
Preamp tubes: 7025 (12AX7A Syl)
Power tubes: 6L6
Tonestack location: PRE
Notes: (make Twin cleaner) “Reduce MV (or MV Trim). Biggest reason Silverface Twins were cleaner is PI had less gain. Reducing MV or MV Trim will simulate lower gain.”

(Original Double Verb model) “Twin Reverb model’s based on breaks up easy. Extremely accurate, a Blackface Twin, breaks up easy vs later ones. As with all models, modeled on Hi input. Don’t want break up as easy? Input Trim 0.5 simulates Low input, cut Input Drive & MV if needed. Can turn Bright Switch off. Those wanting squeaky clean often turn Bright Switch off, but you’d be surprised Twin isn’t nearly as clean as thought, just so damn loud you get sensation it’s clean, as light touch on strings creates lotta volume”
“Model’s extremely accurate & based on 1966 Blackface Twin Reverb. Part of reason Blackface era so sought after is they distort. They’re NOT clean amps by any stretch”

“Dual Showman with AB763 circuit has 1 less gain stage than Twin Reverb, it has less gain. There’s 2 gain stages. Vibrato channel of AB763 Twin Reverb has 3 gain stages. AA769 Dual Showman Reverb Vibrato channel has similar gain, has 3 gain stages. AB763 Dual Showman isn’t same circuit as AB763 Twin Reverb”
“Super Reverb is a Twin Reverb 50w with different speakers”

Twin Reverb may be Fender’s most popular amp. It’s big, loud, takes pedals well & ultra clean. Getting amp into OD requires volume level hardly anyone can handle, unless modded. Twin handles humbuckers well, which isn’t the case with all Fenders

“If aliens came to earth & wanted to hear an American guitar sound, I’d play him my Twin with set of Jensens. This was stated by a happy Twin Reverb owner on Harmonycentral. com. It could have been our own words. The Twin Reverb is the king of Fender blackface amps. Having two pairs of 6L6, the popular & traditional two-channel AB763 circuit design, a diode rectifier & enormous transformers (power & output), it offers tons of clean headroom & volume for unmiked gigs & really big stages. Where the other Fender amps break up at around 4 on the volume knob, the Twin stays clean up to almost 6 & will hold up against heavy drummers & bass players with clean notes sharp as a knife edge. This is exactly what the amp is made for, being played unmiked in a gospel band in a 300-seat church every Sunday. It is designed not break up like the other Fender amps. You need to have your expectations set correctly to be able to appreciate the evil Twin. Warning nr 1) The big transformers & speakers makes this amp weigh around 40kgs, a burden too big for many gigging players who cannot (yet) afford a crew of roadies. Warning nr 2) Volume & clean headroom. If you want tube amp breakup & smoking tones with no pedals you will experience many fights with your band mates & club owners. Just as the other Fender amps the Twin needs to operate in its sweet spot up to sound sweet. After carrying a Twin Reverb on to the stage, the least you should deserve is to crank the bastard. Sadly, we are seldom offered the opportunity to turn the volume above 3 on a Twin Reverb. Instead we have played many nights with a thin & unpleasant tone, even worse with a sparkling, clean sounding strat.” – Fenderguru.com
Blackface Twin Reverb modded by Andy Fuchs & breaks up earlier vs stock Twin. Turn down Input Trim if wanting it cleaner
Tips: Fractal model always based on input with highest input level. Get equivalent of using lower input: set Input Trim 0.5 in amp block
Twin Reverb can be painful bright amp. Can remedy by turning off Bright switch or reduce Bright Cap (try 68pF)
Can produce lot of bass, so don’t hesitate to turn that down
Twin Reverb has 2x12” Jensen speakers, sometimes JBM 120s or EV-12Ls. Try 2x12 Double Verb stock cabs

amps.name: DOUBLE VERB SILVERFACE
body: Model: 1971 silverface Fender Twin Reverb
Orig. Cab: Fender 2x12, original speakers
DynaCab: 2x12 Double Verb
Controls: Bass, Mid, Treble, Bright switch
Preamp tubes: 7025 (12AX7A Syl)
Power tubes: 6L6
Tonestack location: PRE
Notes: Circuit of model unknown, but has no Master Volume
See 123 Double Verb for details
Tips: “When I pick a Double Verb, I choose the Silverface model with Bright off” – yek

amps.name: DOUBLE VERB VIBRATO
body: Model: 1966 blackface Fender Twin Reverb, vibrato channel
Note:See 123 Double Verb for info

amps.name: DWEEZIL’S BASSGUY
body: Model: Fender Bassman, Dweezil Zappa’s ‘65 blackface AB165, modded by Blankenship
Orig. Cab: Fender 2x12 (Oxford)
DynaCab: 4x12 1960TV
Controls: Volume, Negative feedback, fixed Presence circuit (at 10)
Preamp tubes: 7025 (12AX7A Syl)
Power tubes: 6L6
Tonestack location: PRE
Notes: Modded version of Bass Instrument channel & has no tone stack (no tone controls), so you only use Volume control
“Bass Instrument channel is modded, the basis for this model (Dweezil’s B-man). Boatload of gain, sounds more like a Plexi than Fender”
“It’s a crazy sounding amp, sounds nothing like your typical Fender.”

“65 Bassguy was matched to a 65 Bassman Dweezil sent me. It’s a vicious amp, sounds more like Plexi than a Fender, due to infamous AB165 circuit, very crunchy/bright & doesn’t sound like typical Fender. I was told it’s stock. Definitely not your father’s Bassman. Pissed-off Marshall vibe to it”
“Dweezil’s Bassman has 2 channels like a regular Bassman. The Normal channel is “normal” & corresponds to the 65 Bassguy Nrml model.”

Tips: “Actual amp has no tone stack so for maximum authenticity all tone controls would be at noon (don’t be afraid to tweak). In actual amp, Treble control re-purposed as Negative Feedback control. Experiment with different values of Negative Feedback. Lower values are looser.”

amps.name: ENERGYBALL
body: Model: Engl Powerball, Lead channel
Orig. Cab: Engl 4x12 (V30, V60)
DynaCab: 4x12 Recto Straight
Controls: Gain, Lo/Hi Lead, Bass, Mid-Open/-Focus, Treble, Open/Focus, Presence, Depth Punch
Preamp tubes: ECC83
Power tubes: 6L6
Tonestack location: POST

amps.name: EURO BLUE
body: Model: Bogner Ecstasy 20th Anniv., Blue in Vintage mode
Orig. Cab: Bogner 2x12 or 4x12 (V30)
DynaCab: 4x12 Friedman V30
Controls: Presence, Vol, Treble, Middle, Bass, Gain(Drive), 3-way Bright switch, Structure switch, Old/New switch, Excursion switch(Depth), Gain switch, Plexi Mode
Preamp tubes: ECC83
Power tubes: 6L6 (modeled) or EL34
Tonestack location: POST
Notes: Blue=famous crunch. Vintage mode=less gain
Still Bogner flagship amp & Steve Lukather’s current fav
“Fantastic amp. Love for low-mid growl/fat tones” -yek

“Features allow DNA of 3 distinct channels to be radically morphed into sounds of the past, present & future. Imagine going from notorious barking Plexi mode, to the greasiest, harmonically chewy overdrive & then washing it all down with the lushest most decadent clean tone

The Ecstasy comes standard with an amazing array of controls: 1/2 power switch, new/old style switch, dual assignable presence controls with 3-way Excursion switches to control speaker cabinet dampening, gain boost & individual pre-EQ switches for each channel, plexi-mode, gain structure, tube buffered series/parallel FX loop with mix & master control. We consider this the new Flagship in Ecstasy line with most organic & open sound yet. g20th Anniversary Ecstasy has a real plexi panel with gold background & a different pre-amp circuit as well as a foot control able boost feature in the plexi mode. Up until late 2015 the amp was housed in a slightly larger head shell for an iconic & classic vintage vibe. 20th Anniversary model features its own unique circuit & many refinements never before seen.

In the past few years many new boutique capacitors have come on the market such as the Sozo & Jupiter brand which focus on replicating the vintage capacitors from the 60’s. These new capacitors are very expensive & mostly hand made in their construction so we were intrigued to hear how they sound. We purchased all the available brands & meticulously went through the entire signal chain of our Ecstasy circuit incorporating these vintage style capacitors into the design. However, once this process started, we quickly realized that replacing the capacitors was only the beginning, additional adjustments & refinements were implemented to maximize the vintage tonal character of these new capacitors which all together give the 20th Anniversary its iconic Golden-Era of Ecstasy tone. Additional refinements have been made including:

Pre EQs 2&3 shifted, vintage/modern style switch to decide overall flavor of Channel 2/3, bypassed in plexi mode. Plexi channel has slightly more gain & foot controlled boost added, which expands amp to have 6 sounds on tap via foot. Boost engaged with same switch as for Channel 2 & 3. Clean channel has been changed, special emphasis taken to boosted mode, which gives you more gain, with nicely balanced freqs & compression. Power amp dampening adjusted to compliment new preamp design, Excursion switches so to speak. 20th Anniversary amp was designed & features 6L6 power amp tubes standard but can be custom order with EL34s” – Bogner
“My actual XTC has Log10 pot for treble, an odd choice. Problem with log-taper pot is that treble does almost nothing for 1st 50%. So you can’t use your eyes if wanting to copy tone.”
(Class A) “Turn up Power Tube Grid Bias. In real life that is tough on tubes as it makes them run very hot”
“The tonestack tapers in the Ecstasy model match the amp”
“Excursion is Depth. Adjust to taste”
“The Ecstacy uses a switch with three fixed resistor values. The model uses a pot instead. The model is based on a 20th Anniversary addition. It is different than a 101.” [255]
“The values for the L, M & T are 1M, 180K, 0 ohms. The capacitor does not change.”
Tips: Generally considered to be a dark amp, but this can be adjusted through Treble, Presence & Bright
“The models represent the original amp really well. I prefer the non-Modern models, with Presence set high.” – yek
The Bright switch not only adds treble, it adds gain too. At high Gain settings, its effect is less noticeable

“People think Bogners are dark but they really aren’t. The reason they seem that way is the pot tapers. Most people assume knobs should be set somewhere around noon. If you do this on a Bogner it’s like turning the treble way down on a Marshall. Close your eyes & adjust the tone controls with your ears. Don’t be afraid to turn them way up or way down.”

“Human nature is to put the knobs near noon. We are reticent to deviate much from noon. Amp designers exploit this & use different tapers to change the sound of their amps WITH THE KNOBS AT NOON. A prime example are Bogner amps. Everyone says “Bogner amps are dark”. No they aren’t. But he uses a Log10A taper for the treble pot. It’s a standard Marshall tone stack. Usually a linear taper pot is used for the treble. The treble knob at 5.0 (noon) on a Bogner is equivalent to the treble knob at 1.0 on a Marshall. People put the knob at 5.0 & go “wow, this amp is dark”. No it isn’t. If you turned the treble up to 8 or 9 it would sound a lot like a Plexi but humans are reticent to turn the knobs to extremes. Amp designers know this & exploit it to give their amps a “signature sound”.”

“Our reference amp is a 20th anniversary & N is Bright Off, B1 is 150 pF & B2 is 1000 pF for both channels. The models default to B1. Change the Bright Cap value to 1 nF to emulate B2. Or better yet use your ears to select a value.”
Bogner’s cabs range from 1x12 Cube to 4x12 Uberkab with V30 & T75. Bogner Euro stock cabs is logical choice too.
The 1x12 Shiver cab works well too:

“Bogner Blue (non-Modern) is 1 of my all-time, desert island, favorites amps! A reason I did Cab Pack 17 was I had paired my real 101b EL34 Ecstasy amp with 1X12 Bogner Shiva Cab & Classic Lead 80 for a years worth of gigs on 6th street in Austin Texas & loved that tone (was constantly complimented on it), wanted to recreate it with AXE FX. If you have cab pack, check out Bogner 1x12 IRs with XTC amp model & Shiver (Shiva) amp.” - Austinbuddy Turning Amp into Drive Pedal:
Bogner pedals (Ecstasy Blue, Ecstasy Red, Uberschall) are awesome. You have these in your Axe-Fx. Turn equivalent Amp block type into preamp pedal:
Insert additional Amp block before main Amp block
In that block: select Euro Blue, Euro Red or Euro Uber
Turn off power amp sim by setting SAG to 0 (p.a. off)
Set Presence/Hi Cut to “5” (neutral)
Main (last) Amp block should be set to rather clean tone for best results, like using distortion pedal with real amp
Assign switch on your foot controller to 2nd amp’s CC
Should work in 4CM
Another good thing: requires way less CPU vs Drive block

“Reference amp is 20th anniversary, N is Bright Off, B1 is 150pF, B2 is 1000pF for both channels. Models default B1. Change Bright Cap value to 1nF to emulate B2. Better yet use your ears to select a value that sounds best”

amps.name: EURO BLUE MODERN
body: Model: Bogner Ecstasy 20th Anniv. Blue in Modern mode
Notes: Blue is crunch channel, Modern has more gain
See 128 Euro Blue for more details/info

amps.name: EURO RED
body: Model: Bogner Ecstasy 20th Anniv. Red in Vintage mode
Notes: Red is high gain lead channel, Vintage has less gain
See 128 Euro Blue for more details/info

amps.name: EURO RED MODERN
body: Model: Bogner Ecstasy 20th Anniv. Red in Modern mode
Notes: Red is high gain lead channel, Modern=more gain
See 128 Euro Blue for more details/info

amps.name: EURO UBER
body: Model: Bogner Uberschall
Orig. Cab: Bogner 4x12 Uberkab (G12-T75 & V30)
DynaCab: 4x12 Recto Straight
Controls: Gain, Master, Bass, Middle, Treble, Presence
Preamp tubes: ECC83
Power tubes: EL34 (KT88 or 6L6 in real amp)
Tonestack location: POST
Notes: “My Uber is a very early one & probably different than later models. Has feedback of 4-ohm tap, I’ve heard later versions have feedback off 8-ohm tap. To simulate, increase Negative Feedback.”
“Does chugga-chugga better than Thomas the Tank Engine”

“Has massive amounts of negative feedback & RLC resonant network in fdbk. If speaker impedance is highly resonant at same freq as network (as Recto Straight) then power amp becomes oscillator if Presence turned down enough. I suspect real amp behaves the same, but I’m not inclined to lug a Recto cab up here to prove it. Can do the following: Reduce Depth. Reduce Negative Feedback. Use different speaker impedance curve.”
Uberschall was 1 of early really high gain amps. Reinhold Bogner himself calls it: “Armageddon in a box”. Amp has a clean channel, but not what we’re here for, high gain channel is selling point.
Specializes in heavy grinding lows & insane gain
“Uberschall is German word for Super Sonic & we designed this amp primarily for extreme, heavy & aggressive styles of music. Detuned or baritone guitars crave this amp

Unleash mayhem with super aggressive high gain channel, gain, bass, middle, treble, volume & presence controls. You think you’ve heard BASS before, no way, UBERSCHALL crushes on demand. Even extreme gain/volume, channel stays massively focused/resists mushing from heaviest right-hand attack. Power from throaty EL34 output section

Our UBERKAB is perfectly matched for thunderous UBERSCHALL. This 4x12” straight front speaker cab has combo of front loaded Celestion Vintage 30’s & G12T75’s wired 16ohms. This combo unleashes aggressiveness of UBERSCHALL amp & helps max low end & give great cutting power to mids/highs. Our standard Bogner 4x12” cab, with Vintage 30’s, also sounds great with UBERSCHALL, but we wanted to offer a cab fine tuned for flame-throwing UBERSCHALL” – Bogner

Tips: Generates enormous amount of bass, can be overpowering. Turn down Bass & try Cut switch, or put low-gain Drive block in front of Amp block to keep it tight. 2 other important controls: Presence (turn up) & Middle, both have large impact on tone.
“Midrange” control is very interactive with all other tone controls, can go from hollow scooped-mid setting to aggressive in-your-face & on- your-throat kind of intensity.” – Manual
Keep Bass low & engage Cut switch

“Feedback circuit responsible for behavior of Presence & Depth controls. Some Bogners/Diezels have unique type of feedback circuit. There’s no new parameters/nothing to do but twist Presence & Depth knobs to desired tone. No Depth knob, default of Euro Uber model equivalent to amp’s fixed depth circuit.”
Treb more similar to Presence knob. Mid more similar to Treb knob & Presence is a sort of “Contour” knob
Cab: Uberkab 4x12 V30/T75 spkrs, available as stock cab

amps.name: FAS 6160
body: Model: Custom Model (Peavey 5150)
DynaCab: 4x12 5153
Power tubes: 6L6
Notes: “Based on PVH 6160 model, but more open & less fizzy than original amp. Also, virtual choke has replaced resistor found on original’s power supply filter, results in bouncier feel”

“FAS models usually remove/reduce undesirable characteristics of old amps. Guitar tube amp design was nascent in 60s/70s, designers didn’t truly understand ODing tubes. Big issue was blocking distortion (excess bias excursion). When grid conducts, shifts bias point & in old amps, can shift so much tube goes into cutoff prematurely. Modern designs have grid stoppers/smaller coupling caps to reduce bias excursion”

amps.name: FAS BASS
body: Model: Custom model Bass Amp with active controls
DynaCab: 1x15 Portabass
Power tubes: 6550

amps.name: FAS BROOTALZ
body: Model: Custom Aggressive, High-gain Amp Model Some resemblance to Angle Savage
DynaCab: 4x12 5153 Stealth
Power tubes: 6550
Notes: “Amp was an accident, working on Savage model, accidentally used SLO100 input stage, it’s front end of SLO100 with back end of Savage. May be SLO100 power amp too”
“Uses Engl Savage presence network, reduces negative feedback as control’s turned down, causes volume increase”

amps.name: FAS BROWN
body: Model: Custom Model, EVH Brown Sound
DynaCab: 4x12 1960TV
Power tubes: EL34
Notes: FAS take on raw tones of early VH albums

EVH legendary “Brown Sound” probably most sought-after guitar tone: Marshall Superlead Plexi, maybe modded, Variac to run at lower than normal voltage, 4x12 cab JBLs or greenbacks, copious (panned) reverb, EQ magic at mixing
Sound on early VH records is recognizable. Isolate guitar tracks & hear early Eddie didn’t use much gain, tone was very bright

Supposedly Eddie himself said: “There’s a difference between being just loud & having what I call a warm, brown sound, a toney sound. Another story tells brown sound was originally used to describe Alex Van Halen’s drum sound”
Tips: Eddie cranked all amp controls. That sound’s already modeled, don’t have to do it, but there’s no rules, of course
For authenticity, Marshall stocks cabs & greenbacks or JBLs. EVH didn’t use V30s/his customs until later
“For EVH tones, see Danny Danzi posts on forum“-yek

amps.name: FAS BUTTERY
body: Model: Custom FAS Model (v1 AxeFx legacy model)
DynaCab: 2x12 Double Verb
Power tubes: EL84
Notes: “Model by ear, listened to clips of Buddas (Matchbox 20) & tweaked model til I thought it sounded right. Screwed around with tube bias points til it had right harmonic balance”

“Model created by ear. No real amp used. Uses same underlying algorithms for tube modeling. Amp model, regardless of how accuracy (& probably inaccurate, as I never compared to any amp) is very popular & why it wasn’t matched to any real amp”

amps.name: FAS CLASS-A
body: Model: Custom Amp model born by accident
DynaCab: 2x12 Class-A 30W
Power tubes: EL84

Notes: “Blackface” preamp into cathode-biased 6L6 power amp, no negative feedback. Happy accident originally modeling Carr Rambler beta version. Several mistakes made prior to mimicking amp but model was liked, decided to make its own custom amp model”

amps.name: FAS CRUNCH
body: Model: Custom Ultimate British-sound amp model
DynaCab: 4x12 1960TV

Notes: “FAS take on ultimate British-sounding amp. More dynamic/open than Plexi & more gain”

amps.name: FAS EXPRESS
body: Model: Custom Modded Trainwreck Express model
DynaCab: 4x12 Friedman GB
Power tubes: EL34
Notes: Master volume is 1 mod
“My take on modded Trainwreck Express”
(Trainwreck Express) “Exhibits lot of blocking distortion (in phase inverter) due to design
Tips: Can reduce blocking distortion via either PI Bias/Master Bias Excursion parameters
Alt: Use FAS Express, puts resistors between last triode stage/PI, reduces bias excursion”

amps.name: FAS HOT ROD
body: Model: Cliff’s ideal modded Marshall model
DynaCab: 4x12 1960TV
Power tubes: EL34
Notes: “My version of what modded Marshall should be. I find BE/ HBE little too boomy/scooped, Bogners too dark, Splawns don’t have enough compression, etc” - Cliff

amps.name: FAS LEAD 1
body: Model: Custom FAS Model
DynaCab: 4x12 V30 5153 Stealth
Power tubes: 6L6
Notes: Neutral high gain lead tone, tight midrange

amps.name: FAS LEAD 2
body: Model: Custom FAS Model, Hot-rod British lead sound
DynaCab: 4x12 V30 5153 Stealth
Power tubes: EL34
Tonestack location:
Notes: Hot-rodded British lead sound, CAE tonestack

amps.name: FAS MODERN
body: Model: Custom FAS Model, High gain hybrid tone
DynaCab: 4x12 Recto Straight
Power tubes: 6L6
Notes: suited for modern rhythm & lead work. Very popular model, used by Periphery, Andee Blacksugar, etc

“Model is my interpretation of ideal modern metal tone. In digital realm we’re not constrained by limitations tube amp designers face, we’re free to implement designs nearly impossible with a tube amp. AxeFx modeling has a variety of general purpose filters I can put anywhere in signal path. I put some 2nd-order filters to tighten up tone. 2nd-order filters in real tube amp is difficult/costly/rare”

amps.name: FAS MODERN II
body: Model: Custom Model, Tighter FAS Modern version
DynaCab: 4x12 Recto Straight
Power tubes: 6L6
Notes: “Tighter version of popular FAS Modern model with 5150-style bass boost in tone stack”
Tips: “Turn down Presence” – yek

body: Model: Custom Model, like Recto with tighter bass
DynaCab: 4x12 Recto Straight

Notes: Tight bass/cathode-biased power amp
“Turn down Presence” – yek
“Decrease Presence & turn down Drive” – yek

body: Model: Custom Model, Hybrid Marshall/MESA amp
DynaCab: 4x12 1960TV

Notes: “Combines best features of British (Marshall) & USA (Mesa) crunch models”

Notes: “Nastiest, most brutal amp model to ever escape from the 7th circle”
